Cheltenham High School is looking for a Soccer Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER 1.14.25 Cheltenham School District announces the following extra duty-extra pay position is available for the 2026-2027 school-year, at Cheltenham High School:
EDEP: 2026-27 Soccer [Boys] - JV Head Coach
Qualifications Must have extensive knowledge of the sport and the ability to effectively teach and communicate fundamental skills. Must have at least 2 - 4 years of coaching experience working with children and/or adolescents; coaching at the high school level preferred. A strong understanding of the sports theory and practice is required; experience as a player or coach at the high school or collegiate level is preferred. Must demonstrate the personal qualities necessary to foster sportsmanship and support student-athlete success in a positive and encouraging environment. The ideal candidate will possess excellent communication, organizational, and collaboration skills, along with a proven ability to build relationships with student-athletes, parents, staff, and school administration. Applicants must be prepared to submit required background checks that are no more than one year old from the date of issue, in accordance with state law.
All applicants: Must complete the on-line application to be considered for this position, which is located under Employment Opportunities at www.cheltenham.org. External candidates: If hired, you will be required to provide Acts 34, 114, and 151 clearances and other mandatory employment forms.
Position Type: Part-Time Job Requirements Contact Information Nikeya Pressley 2000 Ashbourne Road Elkins Park, Pennsylvania 19027 Phone: (215) 886-9500 Email: npressley@cheltenham.org

Job.JobDescription:
Southeast Delco is looking for a Softball Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Supplemental Contract- 2025-2026 School Year Essential Duties & Responsibilities: Teach and train players on fundamental softball skills such as running, fielding, hitting, throwing, etc. Schedules and attends practices. Makes sure each athlete is cleared from the athletic office to participate in practices and games. Work cooperatively with other head athletic coaches in order to operate a well-balanced athletic program. Collaborate with the Athletic Director and Assistant Coach in preparation of schedules for the team. Recommend to the Athletic Director the type of equipment and supplies needed to conduct the softball program. Assist the Assistant Coach with the inventory and care of equipment. Engage with the parents of the members of the softball team in a positive and constructive manner. Understands the signs and symptoms of injuries such as a concussion and removes players that are exhibiting signs and symptoms of injury. Encourages student athletes to set and maintain high standards of behavior on the field and off. Attends and participates in scheduled staff and team meetings. Supervise and collaborate with any potential volunteers. The Assistant Coach reports to the Head Coach and Athletic Director. Performs other related duties as assigned or requested.
Minimum Qualifications: Demonstration of current knowledge of strength and conditioning programs for softball. Assist with the organization and development of the middle school softball program. Must be team-oriented, hands-on, and passionate about sports. Prior coaching experience desired.
Pre-Employment Requirements: Completion of the following three clearances: PA State Police Background Check, PA Child Abuse Clearance, FBI Fingerprinting Background Check. Completion of PIAA safety and compliance trainings.
Skills/Knowledge/Abilities: The individual must demonstrate the ability to communicate, organize and analyze complex and diverse information and use same to resolve problems in a timely manner. The individual must pursue training and development opportunities in order to continuously build knowledge and skills. Must be willing to share expertise with others. The individual must be able to manage difficult customer situations and respond promptly to customer needs. The individual must be able to solicit customer feedback to improve service, respond to requests for service and assistance and meet commitments. The individual must be able to communicate effectively. Must be able to demonstrate grouppresentation skills. The individual must display openness to others' views, must be able to balance team and individual responsibilities. Must contribute to building positive team spirit by supporting everyone's efforts to succeed. The individual must show respect and sensitivity for cultural differences and work toward promoting a harassment free environment and diverse workforce. The individual must treat everyone with respect, work with integrity and uphold and support Southeast Delco School District's values and goals. The individual must follow all District policies and procedures. T he i nd i v i dua l mus t be a b l e t o p r i o r i t i ze a nd p l an wo r k a c t i v i t i es us i n g t i me e ff i c i en t l y . He o r she mus t r ea c t we l l unde r p r essu r e a nd a ccep t r espons i b i l i t i es f o r h i s / he r own a c t i ons . T he i nd i v i dua l mus t r espond t o manage rnen t d i r ec t i on. The individual must demonstrate accuracy and thoroughness, apply feedback to improve performance and monitor his or her own work to ensure quality. Individual should always be willing to generate suggestions to improve individual and departmental performance. The individual must be punctual at all times and ensure that work responsibilities are covered in his/her absence. Must complete tasks correctly and on time.
Safety Regulations: Must report all student concerns, injuries, and incidents to the Athletic Department immediately. Anyone who knows of any action, inaction, or misconduct are required to file a report with the Athletic Department and shall report such misconduct to his or her school administrator.
Supervisory Responsibilities: Supervise any volunteers associated with the team.
Working Conditions: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. This position requires the incumbent to work both indoors and outdoors in all types of lighting and weather conditions, including but not limited to: heat, sun, rain, snow and ice.
Compensation: Units for the completion of the whole spring season. 1 Unit= $96.00. This position is evaluated on a yearly basis. Evaluations are based on the development of players and the team's overall skillset.
Disclaimer: The preceding list is not exhaustive and may be supplemented as necessary. The statements contained herein reflect general details as necessary to describe the principle functions of this job, the scope of responsibility, and the level of knowledge and skills typically required, but should not be considered an inclusive listing of work requirements, skills or duties so classified. All personnel may be required to perform duties outside their normal responsibilities from time to time as needed.

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Tennis Coach - Cleveland HS - 25/26 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Cleveland HS is looking for a Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Posting Timeline This job is open until filled. Applications will be reviewed as received. Portland Public Schools reserves the right to make a hiring decision at any point during the posting period. Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics. All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings. Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660 Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process

Date Posted: 12/17/2025
Location: Hiroshi Miyamura High School
Date Available: SY2025-2026
Closing Date: Open Until Filled
Title: Athletic Coach – JV Softball Work Location: Hiroshi Miyamura High School Contract Length: Spring Sport Minimum Salary: $5,000.00 Maximum Salary: $5,000.00
*Internal applicants MUST attach a signed Permission to Coach Form to application*
ATHLETIC COACHING LICENSE, 7-12
High School Diploma or Equivalency AND Must hold or have the ability to obtain an Athletic Coaching License (https://webnew.ped.state.nm.us/bureaus/licensure/how-to-apply/athletic-coach-7-12/) from the New Mexico Public Education Department as well as completing the Fundamentals of Coaching online exam offered through the New Mexico Activities Association. (www.nmact.org)
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 2. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. (Major sports) 3. The head coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 4. The head coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions.
REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the high school principals.
SUPERVISES: In several instances, the head coach must advise, coordinate and support a staff of high school assistant coaches and middle school coaches in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal.
JOB GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc.
Work with students interested in sports, provide necessary practice support and advice for a team to be successful against opposing teams in a sports season. Teach and enforce rules and regulations. Involves work on evenings and weekends.

Job.JobTitle: .5 FTE Assistant Track Coach - Caldera High School - Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Bend-LaPine Sch. Dist. is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Bend-La Pine Schools is committed to the principle of equity. Equity supersedes the notion of equality, where all are treated the same. Pursuing equity requires the removal of barriers and the promotion of inclusive practices so that all students fully benefit. The principle of equity will inform all BLS policies, regulations, programs, operations, practices, and resource allocations. Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application.
As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement.
Bend La Pine Schools Assistant Coaching Details
General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Assistant Coach: Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability both behaviorally and academically for all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for your individual discipline. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience in the specific discipline at high school level preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Be involved in fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with head coach and athletic director. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete. Be a supporter of the head coach and the direction they are taking the program. Other duties as assigned by Head Coach & Athletic Director.
Newly hired coaches will be required to complete or show proof of the following certifications prior to employment: 1. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching (one-time requirement, previous completion will be recognized) 2. Concussion in Sports (yearly) 3. Heat Illness Prevention (every 4 years) District Background check 4. Appearance and Performance Enhancing Drugs and Substances Training (every 4 years) 5. Heads up certification (Yearly) (Football only)
Job.Location: Caldera High School, 60925 SE 15th Street, Bend, OR, 97702, United States

Job Description Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: ASSISTANT COACH Reports To: HEAD COACH/PRINCIPAL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES Essential Duties and Responsibilities: 1. Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. 2. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation. 3. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations and implements them consistently. 4. Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperate within the school sports program. 5. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his area of the program. 6. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices and authorizes who may enter. 7. Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned and arranges for issuing and storing of equipment and submits to the head coach an annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. 8. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of the specific equipment. (and so on, including content from 'ASSISTANT COACH (CONT'D)', 'Qualifications', 'Physical Requirements', 'Safety and Health', 'Equipment/Material Handled', 'Work Environment', and 'Job Requirements' sections) Supervisory Responsibilities: Students in his charge. Qualifications: 1. High School diploma or GED. 2. Current NM Coaching licensure including CPR/AED Certification. 3. Valid Driver's license and Car Insurance. 4. Ability to drive from site to site as needed. Physical Requirements: Sitting, standing, lifting and carrying (up to 40 pounds), climbing stairs, reaching, squatting, kneeling, having full mobility of fingers/hands, and moving light furniture may be required, unless ADA accommodations have been mutually agreed on and does not create an undue hardship upon the district. Safety and Health: Knowledge of universal hygiene precautions (blood borne pathogens, body fluids, etc.) Equipment/Material Handled: Must know how to properly operate, or be willing to learn to operate, multi-media equipment including current technology as needed. Work Environment: Must be able to work within various degrees of noise and temperature. Interruptions of work are routine. Flexibility and patience are required. Must be self-motivated and able to complete job assignments without direct supervision. After hours work may be required. May be required to make home and site visits. May work under stressful conditions on occasion. Terms of Employment: Salary and work year to be established by the Board. Job Requirements: High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Job.Location: Mesa Middle School - Athletics - Roswell, New Mexico

Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: ASSISTANT COACH Reports To: HEAD COACH/PRINCIPAL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES Essential Duties and Responsibilities: 1. Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. 2. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation. 3. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations and implements them consistently. 4. Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperate within the school sports program. 5. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his area of the program. 6. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices and authorizes who may enter. 7. Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned and arranges for issuing and storing of equipment and submits to the head coach an annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. 8. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of the specific equipment. 9. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper usage. 10. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. 11. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. 12. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Assists the head coach in scheduling, providing transportation to tournaments and special sporting events. 13. Directs student managers and statisticians on respective games. 14. Provides training rules and any other unique regulation of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 15. Determines student discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary. 16. By his presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance and guidance to and safeguards for each participant. 17. Instructs team members as to changes in the rules and teaches fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the head coach. 18. Assists the head coach in carrying out his responsibility. 19. Attends all staff meetings and carries out scouting assignments as outlined by the head coach. 20. Works within the basic framework and philosophy of the head coach of that sport. 21. Arrives early enough before practice, contests and meetings to adequately prepare and remains long enough afterwards to help players with problems or to become involved in staff discussions. 22. Never criticizes, admonishes or argues with head coach or any staff member within ears or eyes of players or parents. 23. Helps in the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-or-season conditioning and weight programs as is possible. 24. Attends most of the contests, when possible, or other teams in the program. 25. Strives to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the head coach. 26. Performs such other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the head coach. 27. Maintain confidentiality with sensitive matters. 28. Be flexible and able to prioritize tasks and maintain accurate and detailed records. 29. Report to work on time and evening /weekend hours are applicable. 30. Attempt to deescalate students/parents/staff: listening/visiting with them about their concerns prior to them meeting with administrators.

Job.JobDescription:
Deming Public Schools is looking for a Tennis Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
BEGINNING DATE: 25/26 Season
SALARY: $3,300 APPLICATION: IN-DISTRICT: Letter of interest through School Spring - Applicant Tracking. OUT-OF-DISTRICT: Complete application through the School Spring - Applicant Tracking APPLICATION DEADLINE: UNTIL FILLED QUALIFICATIONS: Applicants must have a coaching license or a coaching application with results from the NMAA to apply for their coaching license and a complete application on file.
JOB SUMMARY: To prepare athletes for competition. Participation opportunities are offered at the Varsity, Junior Varsity, 'C' Team, 9th-grade, 8th-grade, and 7th-grade levels of competition for many of the sponsored sports.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Teaching sports techniques, playing rules, strategies, and playing tactics. Conditioning athletes appropriately for activities requiring endurance, strength, and agility. Supervising and conducting practices and contests safely; supervising locker rooms and buses. Teaching sportsmanship, cooperation, work ethic, and responsibility to one's team. Monitoring athletes' school attendance, grades, and personal conduct. Responding to player injuries with approved first aid techniques. Managing uniforms, equipment, and school facilities in a responsible manner. If Major sports course is offered- Expected to actively participate in offseason program development and implementation, including strength and conditioning, skill development, and team activities. Involvement with the school's major sport athletic class during the academic day is required.
SPECIAL CONDITIONS: Vacancies for coaching positions are developed at each school and forwarded to the Associate Superintendent of Human Resources for posting in schools. Candidates for coaching positions are screened and interviewed by the high school athletic director and/or principal. To apply for a coaching job, candidates submit a letter of interest to the office of Human Resources. The athletic director will review all candidate backgrounds and recommend candidates to be interviewed.
SALARY: Coaching salaries vary depending on the coaching assignment and the level of competition (Freshman, Sophomore, JV, or Varsity). Applicants should list related playing or coaching experiences and any training completed. In addition, candidates should list the sports they feel most comfortable teaching/coaching. (List sports in order of capability.) For further information, interested persons should call the Deming Public Schools Director of Activities and Athletics at 575-546-8126.
Revised January 2026

Cincinnati City School District is looking for a Softball Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Responsibilities:
Assist the Varsity Head Coach with the Girls Softball Program. Assist with developing and training student-athletes to participate in play softball. Assist with the development and execution of an effective training program. Monitor, motivate, supervise, and mentor student-athletes on and off the Field. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guild student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, sportsmanship, and skills development.

Job.JobTitle: Coaching - Junior High Asst. Baseball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Cincinnati City School District is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Responsibilities:
Assist with the Junior High Baseball Program. Assist with developing and training student-athletes to play baseball. Assist with the execution and development of an effective training program. Monitor, motivate, supervise, and mentor student-athletes on and off the Field. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guild student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, sportsmanship, and skills development. Meet all requirements set forth by the State Board of Education, CPS, the Ohio High School Athletic Association (OHSAA), and The Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to the mandated Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) and ODE Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Recognize the need to be aware of and implement all applicable administrative policies set forth by the Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season conduct to team members in writing prior to the start of the season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Model the behavior expected of our student-athletes including timeliness to games and practices, being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Be appropriately dressed at all times. Maintain suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Accept and profit from constructive criticism and suggestions for improving coaching performance. Participate in clinics and other available programs to improve coaching performance. Participate in School Events (i.e., Awards Night Programs, Pep Assemblies, Athletic Boosters Meetings, etc.). Show physical vitality for the sport as well as use of good judgement and decision-making. Be competent in techniques of the sport so that student-athletes respond positively to directions given. Demonstrate the ability to adapt properly to changing game situations. Be well-versed and knowledgeable in matters pertaining to the sport and look at ways to constantly improve knowledge and coaching techniques. Be innovative in using new coaching techniques and ideas in addition to already proven methods of coaching. Ensure that team is well-prepared for opponents. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies.
Skills/Essential Functions:
Fundraising experience. Previous experience coaching baseball required. Have a passion and dedication to motivate and inspire student-athletes. Ability to serve as a positive role model. Demonstrated knowledge of skills associated with this sport. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural environment.
Training/Certifications:
Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Successful participation in a similar activity in high school and/or college. Provide a satisfactory result on a County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and FBI Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupils Activity Permit (PAP). Provide proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification
Contract Provisions:

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Tennis Coach - Ida B. Wells HS - 25/26 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Portland Public Sch. is looking for a Tennis Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Details Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice.
The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service.
504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660
Accessibility Statement
Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process
DISTRICT MISSION:
At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions.
DISTRICT PROFILE:
To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265.
Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach:
High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities.
The Head Coach will be responsible for the following:
Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics.
All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment:
NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings.
Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following:
A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference.
All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records.
Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00
* There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts.

Job.JobDescription:
Portland Public Sch. is looking for a Soccer Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics.
All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings.
Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660 Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process

Head Boys Volleyball Coach - E698SY2526 HILLSBORO SD 1J Century High School - Hillsboro, Oregon Open in Google Maps Job Details Job ID:5409998 Application Deadline:Posted until filled Re-Posted:Jan 05, 2026 8:00 AM (UTC)
Starting Date:Immediately
Job Description
E698SY2526 | Head Boys Volleyball Coach | Century High School | Reposted Until Filled
GENERAL DUTIES:
Support the Hillsboro School District athletic objective, policy of interscholastic athletics, and Hillsboro School District Athletic Code. The District believes that extra-curricular activities are an integral part of school life. All students, regardless of their ability, should take part if possible to be able to grow physically, intellectually, and socially through participation. Coaches organize and conduct the athletic programs to meet the needs of the school district and their participants.
ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS:
Experience in and/or knowledge of the sport, coaching techniques, and student supervision. Ability and willingness to effectively communicate in verbal and written modes with student/athletes, parents, assistant coaches, and other school staff. Ability and willingness to perform coaching responsibilities in accordance with school and District procedures and policies. Must have current CPR/First Aid certification. Must have current Concussion Management and other trainings required by the OSAA. Maintain integrity of confidential information relating to students, staff, or District patrons. The ability to work harmoniously with others and collaborate solutions to concerns. Successfully pass background check.
To view the full job description please visit the following link: Head Coach
Position Type:Other Job Requirements Citizenship, residency or work visa required Contact Information Applications Office 3083 NE 49th Place Hillsboro, Oregon 97124 Phone: 503-844-1500 Email: applicationsoffice@hsd.k12.or.us Map

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Boy's Tennis Head Coach - Caldera High School - Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Bend-LaPine Sch. Dist. is looking for a Tennis Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Bend-La Pine Schools is committed to the principle of equity. Equity supersedes the notion of equality, where all are treated the same. Pursuing equity requires the removal of barriers and the promotion of inclusive practices so that all students fully benefit. The principle of equity will inform all BLS policies, regulations, programs, operations, practices, and resource allocations. Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application.
As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement.
Bend La Pine Schools Head Coaching Details
Applicant must submit: 1. Letter of application detailing experience. 2. Current Resume – listing experience related to this position and working with students. 3. A minimum of three letters of recommendation. 4. A minimum of three references with contact information.
General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Head Coach:
Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability academically and behaviorallyfor all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for program. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete.
Job.Location: Caldera High School, 60925 SE 15th Street, Bend, OR, 97702, United States
Job.Experience: Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred

Job.JobDescription:
Wilsonville High School is looking for a Volleyball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assistant Volleyball Coach - 0.5 FTE - Wilsonville High School This position is for 2026-2027 School Year. Start Date: 08/17/2025. Work with the head coach in developing skills, techniques, and strategies for competitive volleyball; monitor student development and academic progress. Instructs players in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of volleyball. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises. Assesses player's skills. Monitors players during competition and practice. Determines game strategy. Supervises athletes during practices and competition. The coach shall have completed the annual online required trainings as well as other West Linn-Wilsonville employment requirements. Community and District Information: The West Linn-Wilsonville School District is nestled within two thriving cities, West Linn and Wilsonville, within the rural area of Clackamas County. Both communities have a strong legacy of support for their schools, music and arts endeavors, and the overall well-being of their patrons. The school district and surrounding communities have experienced steady, constant growth over the past three decades. The estimated student population of the District as of December 2024, is 8,900. It is the policy of the West Linn-Wilsonville Board of Education and School District (WLWV) that no discrimination or harassment on the grounds of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veterans' status, genetic information, or disability shall occur if the employee with or without reasonable accommodation, is able to perform the essential functions of the position. These protections apply to students, employees, and other members of the public. West Linn-Wilsonville School District is committed to equal access and equal opportunity in all activities/services, educational programs, and employment. Persons having questions regarding equal opportunity and nondiscrimination should contact the Director of Human Resources at the West Linn-Wilsonville School District, 503-673-7095. Position Type: Limited Duration

Job.JobTitle: Head Junior High Field Hockey Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Lebanon is looking for a Field Hockey Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Head Junior High Field Hockey Coach
Job Goal: This position involves coaching, mentoring, and guiding student-athletes in individual skill development and team strategies. The coach will also be responsible for fostering a positive and inclusive team culture, ensuring the safety and well-being of players, and always representing the school professionally.
Qualifications: Minimum of 3 years coaching experience (head coaching preferred) Proven coaching techniques and in-depth knowledge, including rules, strategies, and district and PIAA regulations Strong communication, organizational, and leadership skills Ability to foster effective relationships with administrators, staff, students, parents, and governing bodies Commitment to academic and personal development of student-athletes Ability to assess and develop players and coaches Professional demeanor and motivational leadership Effective communicator with strong human relations skills Commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusion Willingness to uphold school policies and work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends Availability to meet with students and parents outside of instructional hours A head coach/assistant coach must be dedicated to organizing and maintaining a structured off-season workout program that supports the ongoing development of their athletes. This involves planning and supervising conditioning, skill-building, and strength training sessions to ensure progress throughout the year and team readiness.
Application Process: All required clearances must comply with the Pennsylvania Department of Education’s mandated criteria for hire. Questions may be directed to the Athletics Department at 717-270-6770 or asinico@lebanon.k12.pa.us All required clearances must comply with the Pennsylvania Department of Education’s mandated criteria for hire.
Application Deadline: The position will remain open until the appropriate candidate is selected.
Job.Experience: Minimum of 3 years coaching experience (head coaching preferred)

Job.JobTitle: Track and Field - Varsity Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
North Hills is looking for a Track & Field Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
NORTH HILLS SCHOOL DISTRICT AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER As required by North Hills Education Association Contract Article XVII, Section B, the following coaching vacancy is posted for the 2025-2026 school year: POSITION: The North Hills School District is seeking a Varsity Head Track and Field Coach QUALIFICATIONS: Demonstrated successful experience coaching at the high school or collegiate level (head coaching experience preferred). Comprehensive knowledge of track and field, meet planning, and program organization. Commitment to fostering academic success, sportsmanship, leadership, and character in student-athletes. Excellent communication and organizational skills. Ability to build positive relationships with students, staff, parents, and community members. All required clearances in accordance with Pennsylvania state law. RESPONSIBILITIES: Lead and oversee all aspects of the varsity track and field program, including staff selection, and meet preparation. Coordinate off-season strength and conditioning programs. Promote the varsity track and field program within the school and community. Ensure compliance with all district, WPIAL, and PIAA policies and regulations. SALARY: Minimum $5,600 annual stipend with increase based upon previous coaching experience
Job.SalaryRange: Minimum $5,600 annual stipend with increase based upon previous coaching experience

Dutchman Creek Middle School - Rock Hill is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources. PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training. Position Type: Seasonal Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching

Jenks Public Schools is looking for a Volleyball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Jenks Public Schools Job Description
Qualifications:
1. High School Diploma/GED required.
2. One year of experience in coaching or participation at the high school or college level preferred.
3. Evidence of ability to work with student athletes in volleyball preferred.
4. Oklahoma CDL with P & S endorsements or ability to obtain one.
5. Ability to work with others in a positive productive manner.
6. Education with integrity and respect.
7. Satisfy all district requirements with regard to physical health and citizenship.
8. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable.
Position Goal: Assist with all aspects of student-athlete development and performance of the volleyball program.
Essential Functions and Responsibilities:
1. Assist in the planning, development, and the execution of the high school boys volleyball program by focusing on building a positive culture and developing quality relationships with student athletes and parents.
2. Assist in the implementation of sound practices and skill development that maximizes student athlete performance in volleyball in collaboration with the head coach.
3. Communicate effectively with all program stakeholders to ensure a positive, quality experience is provided for our student athletes.
4. Collaborate with the coordinator, assistant coordinator and the Booster Club.
5. Assist in providing direction and evaluation of youth coaches and teams to develop vertical alignment throughout the program.
Employment Terms: Community Education Classified - Temporary Contract
FLSA Qualification: Non-Exempt
Evaluation: None
Physical Demands: This is an overview intended for compliance with the ADA – it is not an exhaustive list of the duties to be performed. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals to perform the essential functions of a specific position.
Regular Requirements: Stand for extended periods; sit for extended periods; twist at the neck and trunk; bend at the waist; stoop, kneel, crouch; lift/move up to 20 pounds; handle objects and tools with hands; repetitive use of fingers and hands.
Occasional Requirements: Reach with hands and arms overhead, above shoulder and horizontally; lift/move up to 75 pounds.
Visual Requirements: Close, color, peripheral, depth perception, ability to adjust focus.
Hearing/Speaking Requirements: Adequate to exchange information. Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Head JVIII Volleyball Coach | Open Until Filled
GENERAL DUTIES: Support the Hillsboro School District athletic objective, policy of interscholastic athletics, and Hillsboro School District Athletic Code. Job.JobDescription:
Bend-LaPine Sch. Dist. is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit; any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement.
Bend La Pine Schools Assistant Coaching Details
General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Assistant Coach:
Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability both behaviorally and academically for all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for your individual discipline. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience in the specific discipline at high school level preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Be involved in fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with head coach and athletic director. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete. Be a supporter of the head coach and the direction they are taking the program. Other duties as assigned by Head Coach & Athletic Director.
Newly hired coaches will be required to complete or show proof of the following certifications prior to employment:
1. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching (one-time requirement, previous completion will be recognized) 2. Concussion in Sports (yearly) 3. Heat Illness Prevention (every 4 years) District Background check

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Tennis Coach - Benson HS - 25/26 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Portland Public Sch. is looking for a Tennis Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Posting Timeline This job is open until filled. Applications will be reviewed as received. Portland Public Schools reserves the right to make a hiring decision at any point during the posting period. Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics. All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings. Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660

Assist in promoting high ideals in sportsmanship, appropriate student behavior, a healthy life style and to serve as a positive role model for the student athletes. 3. Ability to relate/communicate in a positive manner to the student athlete. 4. Acceptance and execution as specified duties as assigned by the Head Coach. 5. Any other duties assigned by the Superintendent or his designee. POSITION SPECIFICATIONS: PHYSICAL DEMANDS: 1. Ability to sit and talk and hear. 2. Ability to stand; walk; climb or balance; stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl and move quickly when necessary to promote student learning and/or safety. 3. Ability to lift and/or move up to 50 pounds. 4. Ability to push items of 50 lbs such as pushing children in a wheelchair or moving/rearranging furniture. SENSORY ABILITY: 1. Ability to speak clearly and distinctly. 2. Auditory acuity to be able to use telephone. 3. Visual acuity to read correspondence and computer screen. WORK ENVIRONMENT: 1. Indoor and outdoor environments. TEMPERAMENT: 1. Ability to work as a member of a team. 2. Must be courteous. 3. Must be cooperative, congenial, service oriented, and promote these qualities in the building. 4. Ability to work in an environment with frequent interruptions. COGNITIVE ABILITY: 1. Ability to follow written and verbal directions. 2. Ability to demonstrate good written and verbal skills. 3. Ability to effectively present information in one-on-one and small group situations to students, staff, and other employees of the organization.
Job.Location: Millcreek Township School District, Erie, Pennsylvania

Job.JobDescription:
HIGH SCHOOL - PERKASIE is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Details Title: Assistant Coach - Unified Track & Field Location: Available: ASAP Reports to: Athletic Director Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a motivated and knowledgeable individual to serve as an Assistant Coach for our Unified Track & Field program. This role supports the Head Coach in providing leadership, mentorship, and instruction to student-athletes while promoting a positive, inclusive team culture. Key Responsibilities: Assist the Head Coach with planning and leading practices and competitions. Support the development of student-athletes' skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Promote academic achievement and personal growth among athletes. Assist with supervision and safety of all team members during activities. Help manage equipment and maintain team standards of conduct. Model positive behavior and represent the school in a professional manner. Follow all school, district, league, and state athletic association rules. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent. Some coaching or playing experience in the sport (preferably at the high school level). Basic understanding of the game, rules, and strategy. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with the Head Coach and school staff. Preferred Qualifications: CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience working with youth or student-athletes. Familiarity with school athletic program policies and procedures. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements: Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB test within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates applying for the above position should submit an online application. EOE Position Type: Coach / Extra Duty Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching Job Requirements: High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Job.JobDescription:
Lebanon is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Varsity Assistant Track & Field Coach Job Goal: This position involves coaching, mentoring, and guiding student-athletes in individual skill development and team strategies. The coach will also be responsible for fostering a positive and inclusive team culture, ensuring the safety and well-being of players, and always representing the school professionally. Qualifications: Minimum of 3 years coaching experience (head coaching preferred) Proven coaching techniques and in-depth knowledge, including rules, strategies, and district and PIAA regulations Strong communication, organizational, and leadership skills Ability to foster effective relationships with administrators, staff, students, parents, and governing bodies Commitment to academic and personal development of student-athletes Ability to assess and develop players and coaches Professional demeanor and motivational leadership Effective communicator with strong human relations skills Commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusion Willingness to uphold school policies and work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends Availability to meet with students and parents outside of instructional hours A head coach/assistant coach must be dedicated to organizing and maintaining a structured off-season workout program that supports the ongoing development of their athletes. This involves planning and supervising conditioning, skill-building, and strength training sessions to ensure progress throughout the year and team readiness. Application Process: All required clearances must comply with the Pennsylvania Department of Education’s mandated criteria for hire. Questions may be directed to the Athletics Department at 717-270-6770 or asinic@lebanon.k12.pa.us All required clearances must comply with the Pennsylvania Department of Education’s mandated criteria for hire. Application Deadline: The position will remain open until the appropriate candidate is selected. The Lebanon School District is an equal-opportunity employer.

Job.Experience: Minimum of 3 years coaching experience (head coaching preferred)

Beaufort County is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: Prepares each participating student athlete for competition in sports activities, and helps them to achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: • Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. • Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. • Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. • Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. • Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. • Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. • Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. • Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: • Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. • Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. • Teaching certificate and experience preferred. • Must be able to pass criminal background check. • Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE: • Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. • Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. • Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. • Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: 1. Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. 2. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. 3. Speak and hear. 4. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. 5. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. 6. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: 1. Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. 2. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. 3. Have regular and punctual attendance. 4. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. 5. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Athletic Director DAYS PER YEAR: To be Determined SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Paid on the Stipend Schedule EMPLOYEE GROUP: Part-Time Temporary APPLICATION PROCEDURES: Must apply online by completing the appropriate application and adding this specific job to your online application. FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt EMPLOYEE'S ACKNOWLEDGEMENT STATEMENT:
Job.Experience: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport.

Rock Hill is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration
Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants.
Key Responsibilities: * Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. * Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. * Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. * Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. * Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. * Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. * Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. * Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. * Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. * Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. * Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes.
Qualifications: * Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. * Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. * Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. * Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. * Satisfactory background report
Preferred: * Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. * Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.)
Start Date: Determined by Human Resources
PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs.
EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.
Job Requirements: * Citizenship, residency or work visa required

ATHLETIC AND ACTIVITY DIRECTOR Reports To: Deputy Superintendent Salary: Administrative Calendar: 240 Days
JOB SUMMARY The Athletic and Activity Director is responsible for the administration, supervision, and coordination of all district-sanctioned athletic and student activity programs for grades 7–12. The position provides leadership in developing, implementing, and sustaining a comprehensive, educationally sound athletics and activities program that aligns with district goals, Board policy, and New Mexico Activities Association (NMAA) requirements. QUALIFICATIONS Valid New Mexico Administrative License. Master’s Degree in education or related field preferred. Minimum of five years of successful teaching and/or administrative experience. Minimum of five years of successful coaching or athletic administration experience preferred. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Superintendent may find appropriate and acceptable. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Program Leadership and Development Compliance and Eligibility Scheduling, Events, and Travel Budgeting and Financial Oversight Facilities and Event Operations Records, Recognition, and Communication Professional Responsibilities The physical demands described are representative of those required to perform the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, stand, walk, use hands to finger, handle, or feel, and talk or hear. Ability to lift and/or move up to 25 pounds Visual abilities including close, distance, color, peripheral, and depth perception WORKING CONDITIONS Mental Demands/Physical Demands/Environmental Factors: Maintain emotional control under stress, work with frequent interruptions. Some districtwide and statewide travel. Occasional, prolonged and irregular working hours. TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT Salary and work year shall be in accordance with the current administrative schedule. This is a 240-day contract. EVALUATION Performance will be evaluated in accordance with Board of Education policy on administrator evaluations. Note: This is not an exhaustive or all-inclusive list of responsibilities, skills, duties, requirements, efforts, functions or working conditions associated with the job. This job description is not a contract of employment or a promise or guarantee of any specific terms or conditions of employment. The school district may add to, modify or delete any aspect of this job (or the position itself) at any time as it deems advisable. Revised February 2026 THE DEMING PUBLIC SCHOOL DISTRICT IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER AND DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE ON THE BASIS OF RACE, NATIONAL ORIGIN, RELIGION, AGE, SEX, MARITAL STATUS, OR HANDICAP IN COMPLIANCE WITH FEDERAL AND STATE LAWS Position Type: Full-Time Job Requirements Citizenship, residency or work visa required
