Job.JobDescription: l class='postingsList' id='p34919_38856'> AE Welding Instructor JobID: 34919 Position Type: Adult Education/ Adult Education - Nursing Date Posted: 2/27/2026 Location: Upper Valley Career Center Adult Technology Center Closing Date: 06/30/2026 District: Upper Valley Career Center Additional Information: Show/Hide General Job Description: Part-time Evening Instructors needed. Applicants can teach just one or several courses. Each class is approximately 4-5 weeks in length, 2 nights a week. Upper Valley Career Center Adult Technology Center is seeking to fill a vacancy for a Welding Instructor with GMAW/GTAW/SMAW and Plasma cutting knowledge. Part-Time Instructors are an integral part of the Adult Technology's staff, helping us provide all learners with the highest quality learning opportunities when, where and how they want it. Responsibilities: Responsibilities include but are not limited to the following: Assists program supervisor with curriculum development, course scheduling and Program Advisory Committee meetings. Plan, prepare, and execute training strategies by facilitating classes and providing technical assistance, and/or individual coaching. Work collaboratively with employers to ensure the curriculum and learning outcomes prepare employees/students for success at work. Determine the appropriate tools, techniques, methodologies, and timing of improvement activities to meet business objectives. Develop customized participant training materials, active learning activities, and events. Deliver customized Welding courses to include Mig, Tig, and Stick welding. Manage classroom and shop/lab to ensure a productive and safe learning environment. Work closely with business leadership to develop key performance measurements. Strong organizational skills, interpersonal skills, teamwork, and ability to prioritize multiple tasks while remaining customer-focused. Ability to work independently, remain flexible in a fast-paced environment and handle customer issues in a timely manner. Qualifications: At least five years of direct occupational experience. At a minimum, occupational experience has to have occurred at least three years within the last five years. Strong organizational skills, interpersonal skills, teamwork, and ability to prioritize multiple tasks while remaining customer-focused. Ability to work independently, remain flexible in a fast-paced environment, and handle customer issues in a timely manner. Must hold a valid driver's license and be insurable under the District's standard insurance policy terms. Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. Competency in adult-focused learning. Meets all mandated health-screening requirements. Holds or is able to obtain a valid Ohio State Board of Education Adult Education Permit . Maintains a record free of criminal violations that would prohibit public school employment. Able to successfully pass a BCI/FBI background check. Maintains compliance with drug-free workplace rules, Board policies and other applicable procedures/guidelines. Salary: Appropriate placement on the Adult Education Intermittent/Hourly Salary Schedule to be determined by education and experience. Compensation is $25.00 per hour for In-House Training and $30.00 per hour for Off-Site Training, on an as-needed basis. Maximum 839 working hours in an academic year. One-year contract with opportunity for renewal. Other Benefits: State Teachers Retirement System and Worker's Compensation offered. Upon hire, additional benefits may be offered depending upon hours worked, job classification, and placement on the Upper Valley Career Center Salary Schedule. Some benefits are a shared cost between the employee and the Board of Education. Performance Evaluation: Job performance is evaluated according to policy provisions and contractual agreements adopted by the Upper Valley Career Center. This job posting description identifies general responsibilities and is not intended to be a complete list of all duties

Job.JobDescription: ass='postingsList' id='p34973_38846'> Boys Varsity Soccer Coach JobID: 34973 Position Type: Athletics/Activities Date Posted: 3/4/2026 Location: Preble Shawnee High School Date Available: 04/01/2026 Closing Date: 03/15/2026 District: Preble Shawnee Local Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION: Athletic Coach REPORTS TO: Athletic Director or Designee EMPLOYMENT STATUS: Seasonal, FLSA Exempt QUALIFICATIONS: High school diploma or equivalent Valid Ohio Certification/Licensure by the State of Ohio, Department of Education Previous experience as a coach or player in the specific sport(s) being coached. Strong knowledge of the rules, strategies, and techniques of the sport(s) being coached. Excellent communication, leadership, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and build positive relationships with student-athletes. Understanding of and commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in athletics. Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with students, teachers, and parents. Patience, empathy, and a genuine interest in supporting student development. Ability to follow directions, maintain confidentiality, and uphold professional standards. Valid Ohio Driver's License Must pass criminal background check Ohio Department of Education Pupil Activity Permit (PAP) CPR, First Aid, and AED Certification Fundamentals of Coaching (NFHS) Concussion in Sports (NFHS) Sudden Cardiac Arrest Training (Lindsay's Law) GENERAL DESCRIPTION: The Boys Varsity Soccer Coach is responsible for leading, developing, and mentoring student-athletes in a competitive high school soccer program. The coach promotes skill development, teamwork, sportsmanship, academic accountability, and character while aligning with school and athletic department policies. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Plan, organize, and implement daily practices and game strategies. Teach fundamental soccer skills and advanced techniques appropriate for high school athletes. Supervise and mentor assistant and JV coaches. Monitor academic performance and eligibility of student-athletes. Promote character development, discipline, and sportsmanship. Maintain equipment and manage inventory. Organize team transportation, game scheduling, and communication with parents and school personnel. Represent the school positively at all athletic and community events. Ensure compliance with school policies, OHSAA regulations, and safety standards. Collaborate with the Athletic Director for scheduling, budgeting, and program needs. Attend all required meetings, trainings, and professional development. OTHER DUTIES AND FUNCTIONS: Serves as a role model for students in how to conduct themselves as citizens and as responsible, intelligent human beings. Conducts and presents the position in a professional manner. Maintains a positive work attitude. Effectively uses verbal, nonverbal, writing, and listening skills. Ability to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as required. Physical ability to conduct practices and coach during games. Travel may be required for away games and tournaments. Actively seeks to acquire knowledge and skills to meet new challenges through involvement in professional development activities. Is punctual and thorough in implementation of all assigned duties. KNOWLEDGE OF: Board policies and procedures, building policies and procedures, student code of conduct, community resource agencies, emergency preparedness drills, public relations, federal and state reporting, and records management. ABILITY TO: Interpret policies, procedures and regulations, follow instructions, communicate effectively, prepare, maintain, and file accurate and concise reports, collect data, recognize individual differences, lift a moderate amount of weight, develop a rapport with students, staff, co-workers, administration and the public. ADDITIONAL WORKING CONDITIONS AND ENVIRONMENT: OCCASIONAL: Exposure to blood, bodily fluids and tissue, lifting (10-5
Job.CompanyDescription: Kettering City School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: st' id='p6080_79074'> High School Assistant Boys Baseball Coach JobID: 6080 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Athletic Coach Date Posted: 2/24/2026 Location: Springfield High School Date Available: 2025/26 SY Closing Date: 03/08/2026 District: Springfield City Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Who We Are: The Springfield City School District, home to The John Legend Theatre, is seeking a High School Assistant Boys Baseball (JV) Coach/Advisor for the 2025-26 school year Spring Season. We are a nationally ranked Division I District serving approximately 7,500 Pre-K through grade 12 students, in a vibrant learning community of 17 state of the art buildings in west-central Ohio. The Springfield City School District values the racial, ethnic and socioeconomic diversity of our student body, faculty and staff. Our academic and extracurricular aim is to ensure social and emotional learning growth for students with a wealth of opportunity, including a 1:1 initiative and the YouMedia Center, which is a digital learning lab offering students activities such as 3-D printing, graphic design, and videography. We are Wildcats! As a Coach you are required to: Completion of an approved CPR course issued by the American Red Cross, The American Heart Association or similar entity. Completion of a 4-hour Sports Medicine or Sports Safety First Aid course issued by the American Red Cross or similar entity. Completion of an 8-hour Fundamentals of Coaching course as required. Completion of the Concussion in Sports course as required. Completion and cerfitication on ODE of the Lindsay's Law video as required. Must possess or have the ability to obtain a Pupil Activity Permit as issued by the Ohio Department of Education As a Coach you will demonstrate the following: Being a role model and mentor to student athletes Ability to build strong communication links between students, parents and the community Ability to build strong connections with collegiate level contacts both in academics and recruiting Action plans to create a disciplined team, both with coaches and student athletes What We Offer: As a High School Assistant Baseball Coach, the stipend is $3,821.00 All "Requirements for Coach/Advisor" must be met on or before the first practice date and throughout the entire season of sport to be performed. Copies of the above documents must be on file in Human Resources. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 High School Assistant Boys Tennis Coach
Job.CompanyDescription: Kettering City School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: id='p34440_38784'> Head 7th and 8th Grade Football Coach - Boys JobID: 34440 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 1/9/2026 Location: Beavercreek City Schools Date Available: Fall 2026 District: Beavercreek City Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Applications are now being accepted for a Head 7th/8th Grade Football Coach - Fall - Scale 6 Salary range of $3,318.00 - $5,097.00 to be paid in 2 pays 2026-2027 School Year All applicants must possess a valid Ohio Certificate/License/Permit in the appropriate area. Proper athletic certification is required for this assignment, if hired. These can be submitted with your application if complete. Applicants who are not yet certified may apply. Coaches ToolChest - https://coachestoolchest.com/ - $49 fee; will certify you in all needed areas including Fundamentals of Coaching, First Aid for Coaches, Concussion Training, CPR Training, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest Training. This will need to be renewed yearly. Apply for or submit a Pupil Activity Permit (PAP) - through the Ohio Department of Education. Price varies depending on the length of permit you qualify for; typically a 3 or 5 year permit. If you do not have an OH-ID login, one must be created for initial access. For more details, please visit the ODE website: https://education.ohio.gov/Topics/Teaching/Licensure/Audiences/Coaching-Permits Complete a background check if hired - through Beavercreek City Schools board office - $47.25 fee; cash, check or charge. This can be scheduled anytime by clicking here: Background Check Calendar . 4029 Executive Drive, Beavercreek, OH 45430. Thank you for your interest! Beavercreek City Schools Athletic Department Attachment(s): Enter file description Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Assistant Varsity Football Coach

Job.JobDescription: List' id='p34909_38862'> Volleyball Coach 8th grade - 2026-2027 JobID: 34909 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 2/26/2026 Location: Warner Middle School Date Available: 08/01/2026 Closing Date: 03/12/2026 District: Xenia Community Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide The vacancy posted is in compliance with the negotiated agreement between the Xenia Education Association and the Xenia Board of Education. All interested applicants need to apply online. Once hired you will need to complete the certifications below: 1. Coaches Tool Chest will certify you in all needed areas including Fundamentals of Coaching, mental health, First Aid for Coaches, Concussion Training, PBIS, CPR Training, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest Training. 2. Apply for or submit a Pupil Activity Permit - through the Ohio Department of Education. 3. Complete a background check - at the Xenia Board of Education fee of $52.00(cash, debit/credit card). Please call 937-562-9037 - to schedule an appointment. Thank you for your interest in Xenia Community Schools Athletics. Job.JobDescription: ss='postingsList' id='p34471_38839'> Varsity Girls Tennis Coach JobID: 34471 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 1/15/2026 Location: Dixie High School Closing Date: 03/30/2026 District: New Lebanon Local Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Varsity Girls Tennis Head Coach for the 2026 Fall Season Dixie High School is accepting applications for Varsity Girls Tennis Head Coach Interested candidates should email a cover letter, resume (teaching certification attached if applicable), references and a brief statement of your philosophy on coaching to Athletic Director Theo Whitaker at twhitaker@newlebanonschools.org. Description : Work with athletes to improve personal and teamwork skills. Provide guidance and encouragement to help student profit from their participation. Promote school spirit and participation in athletic activities as vital aspects of the district's educational mission. Recognize each contact with the public as an opportunity to promote a positive impression of the school district. Actively encourage parental involvement in the athletic program. Qualifications: A clear record as determined by the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Identification (BCI) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI). Coaching expertise in the assigned sport. Completion of all pupil activity supervisor validation requirements. Documentation of current cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) training. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Boys and Girls Varsity Golf Coach

Job.JobDescription: lass='postingsList' id='p34137_38834'> Varsity Volleyball Coach JobID: 34137 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 11/19/2025 Location: Milton-Union High School Date Available: 05/01/2026 District: Milton Union Exempted Village Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Milton-Union Schools — Head Varsity Volleyball Coach Milton-Union High School is seeking a Head Varsity Volleyball Coach for the upcoming 2026–2027 school year. We are looking for a dedicated coach who can provide leadership, foster player development, and continue building a competitive and positive program. Qualifications: Prior volleyball coaching experience preferred Strong communication and organizational skills Ability to develop student-athletes on and off the court Must meet all OHSAA coaching requirements, including: Valid Pupil Activity Permit (PAP) Current FBI and BCI background checks Responsibilities Include: Leading all aspects of the varsity volleyball program Building a summer training program and camp schedule that prepares athletes for the upcoming season Fostering a positive and competitive team culture Communicating effectively with players, families, and school administration How to Apply: Interested candidates should send a letter of interest and resume to Athletic Director Chris Fulton at fultonc@muschools.com Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 High School Track Program Assistants

Job.JobDescription: ostingsList' id='p34769_38834'> Head Varisty Girls Soccer Coach JobID: 34769 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 2/20/2026 Location: Milton-Union High School Date Available: 06/01/2026 District: Milton Union Exempted Village Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Milton-Union High School is seeking a highly motivated and student-centered individual to serve as Head Varsity Girls Soccer Coach. This position will oversee all aspects of the girls soccer program and work closely with the athletic department to promote excellence in academics, athletics, and character development. Responsibilities: Lead, organize, and manage the varsity girls soccer program Develop student-athletes both on and off the field Plan and conduct practices, games, and offseason programs in compliance with OHSAA regulations Promote sportsmanship, teamwork, and leadership Communicate effectively with athletes, parents, staff, and administration Maintain equipment and facilities in coordination with the athletic department Qualifications: Previous coaching experience preferred Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills Commitment to student-athlete development and education-based athletics Must meet all OHSAA, state, and district coaching certification requirements Must obtain and maintain a valid Ohio Pupil Activity Permit Must pass BCI and FBI background checks Application Process: Interested candidates should submit a letter of interest, resume, and references to: Chris Fulton Athletic Director Milton-Union High School fultonc@muschools.com Application deadline: Until Filled Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Assistant Varsity Boys Tennis Coach

Job.JobDescription: ngsList' id='p34491_38849'> Assistant Varsity Boys Tennis Coach JobID: 34491 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 1/20/2026 Location: Springboro Community City Schools District: Springboro Community City Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Assistant Varisty Boys Tennis Coach SPRINGBORO HIGH SCHOOL SUMMARY: This person will be employed on a one (1) year supplemental contract, which expires at the end of the school year, to serve as an Assistant Boys Tennis Coach. Responsible in assisting supervising student activities, and following all district/building policies associated with this activity. Reports to Athletic Director. DUTIES AND PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS: 1. Responsible and assists all practice sessions and scheduled games. 2. Work in cooperation with other staff members and Athletic Director. 3. Distribute, collect and inventory all equipment/uniforms. 4. Keep all necessary records and forms. 5. Provide leadership and direction to students involved in this activity. 6. Promote school and student pride by requiring players to behave in an appropriate and respectful manner when dealing with officials, players, coaches, spectators or any other school personnel. 7. Maintain locker room facilities in a clean and presentable manner. Will be responsible to check locker room after each practice/meet and report any concerns. 8. Promote sportsmanship and goodwill at all times. 9. Perform other functions as necessary to implement goals and objectives of the athletic programs. 10. Coaching activities on school grounds are limited to this supplemental unless another supplemental contract is secured. (This includes camps and other individual programs that could directly benefit the coach.) 11. Each coach shall support the team's fundraising efforts by organizing volunteer staffing of the concession stands and lead by example by co-participating in concession operations while the team's volunteers are working. At least one coach shall be present for the entire shift each time the team's volunteers are working. 12. Perform other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Valid teaching certificate preferred. 2. Prior experience and/or knowledge of the game preferred. 3. Ability to work with those involved in setting up the program. 4. Ability to work with young athletes and provide a positive influence on their lives. 5. Maintain current Pupil Activity Permit validation, CPR training certificate, and other certificates as required by Board Policy and the Ohio Department of Education. 6. Must pass criminal background check. CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT: 1. Effective Date: 2025/2026 school year 2. Length of Employment: One-year supplemental contract. 3. Salary: According to the negotiated agreement. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Asst. Softball Coach – Middle School

Job.JobDescription: le class='title' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> Teacher on Special Assignment - Behavioral Support Coach 2026-2027 JobID: 34685 Position Type: High School Teaching/ Intervention Specialist Date Posted: 2/18/2026 Location: Mad River Central Office District: Mad River Local Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Teacher on Special Assignment - Behavioral Support Coach 2026-2027 Benefits Available: Medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and graduate level tuition reimbursement. MUST HOLD PROPER LICENSURE - Intervention Specialist License The on-site Behavioral Support Coach (TOSA) provides direct, embedded support to staff and students with a focus on Autism Spectrum Disorder, and low-incidence disabilities. This position is not office-based, it is hands-on, in classrooms, and in buildings, working side-by-side with educators to strengthen instructional practices, behavior supports, and inclusive systems. Responsibilities - Provide on-site coaching and professional development for staff with students who have Autism, low-incidence disabilities, and other challenges. Assist with behavior intervention plans (BIPs) and functional behavior assessments (FBAs). Support proactive behavior strategies and de-escalation practices. Collaborate with teams during times of increased student need or transition. Model effective behavioral, and instructional strategies in real time. Coach teachers, paraprofessionals, and support staff in inclusive practices. Provide feedback that is practical, respectful, and immediately usable. Work closely with special education teams, administrators, counselors, and related service providers. Participate in IEP meetings as needed to support programming and implementation. Help identify trends and needs across buildings to strengthen district practices. Maintain clear documentation and communication with district leadership. Mad River Local Schools is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and adheres to all applicable federal and Ohio state laws, regulations, and executive orders concerning non-discrimination. All applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including gender identity, sexual orientation, and pregnancy), national origin, age, disability, or genetic information. If an applicant with a disability needs a reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, the Mad River Board of Education will ensure such accommodations are made in compliance with federal and state legal requirements. Job.JobDescription: gsList' id='p34626_38862'> Fall Percussion Director - 2026-2027 JobID: 34626 Position Type: High School Teaching/ Music - Band Date Posted: 2/11/2026 Location: Xenia High School Date Available: July 2026 Closing Date: UNTIL FILLED District: Xenia Community Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide The vacancy posted is in compliance with the negotiated agreement between the Xenia Education Association and the Xenia Board of Education. All interested applicants need to apply online. GENERAL DESCRIPTION: Under the Supervision of the High School Band Director, the Fall Percussion Director is responsible for the planning and instruction of the school's percussion students during the fall marching band season (July to November). The instructor prepares appropriate music and rehearses the ensemble for events. QUALIFICATIONS High School Diploma Strong background in marching band, and percussion performance. Drum corps or university marching band experience preferred. Experience in the various subgroups of the percussion section (e.g. snare, bass, tenors, mallets, etc.) Previous experience with instructing/teaching percussion. Experience working with high school-aged students. Ability to inspire students, building enthusiasm and love of the subject Ability to tune/replace drum heads and do minor repairs on drumline equipment. Ability to work effectively with students, staff, parents, administrators, and community members. Maintain a high level of ethical behavior and exercise good judgment in making decisions. Strong organizational and communication skills Ability to interact with students of different ages and skill levels positively and professionally. Be responsible for the selection, training, and performances at designated events. See attached for the full job description You will need to complete the certifications below once hired. 1. Coaches ToolChest will certify you in all needed areas including Fundamentals of Coaching, mental health, First Aid for Coaches, Concussion Training, PBIS, CPR Training, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest Training. 2. Apply for or submit a Pupil Activity Permit - through the Ohio Department of Education. 3. Complete a background check - at the Xenia Board of Education fee of $52.00(cash, debit/credit card). Please call 937-562-9037 - to schedule an appointment. Thank you for your interest in Xenia Community Schools Music Department.. Job.JobDescription: ist' id='p27796_42451'> District Gifted Intervention Specialist JobID: 27796 Position Type: Middle School Teaching/ Gifted Intervention Specialist Date Posted: 2/27/2026 Location: Greeneview Board of Education Date Available: 08/01/2026 Closing Date: 03/13/2026 District: Greeneview Local School District Additional Information: Show/Hide Greeneview Local Schools is looking for a District Gifted Intervention Specialist for the 2026-2027 school year. Information on the position is available from Sabrina Woodruff, Superintendent, at sabrina.woodruff@greeneview.org . Applicant must demonstrate: The ability to effectively plan and teach lessons that support and extend the New State Learning Standards and meet the needs of gifted learners The ability to make and carry out long-range instructional and program planning An understanding of best practices for quality instruction and student achievement The ability to differentiate instruction to accommodate learners with multiple ability levels in the regular classroom and small group settings The ability and willingness to work as part of a team of classroom teachers and administrators to plan how to best meet the needs of students. The willingness to be an instructional coach and resource for classroom teachers, consulting and providing information and suggestions for strategies, modifications, and materials that will challenge gifted students and maximize their achievement The willingness to be a resource for parents of gifted students The ability to effectively communicate with students, parents, and teachers The ability to keep accurate and thorough records A comprehensive understanding of laws regarding gifted education, including identification practices and procedures An understanding of multiple delivery models for gifted education An understanding of student reporting for the State EMIS A record of consistently managing paperwork and handling work assignments in a timely manner The ability to support whole grade and individualized gifted testing A willingness to provide professional learning to teachers Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Intervention Specialist - 2026-2027 School Year

Job.JobDescription: 'p34433_38834'> Intervention Specialist - 2026-2027 School Year JobID: 34433 Position Type: Middle School Teaching/ Intervention Specialist Date Posted: 1/8/2026 Location: Milton-Union Middle School Date Available: 08/18/2026 District: Milton Union Exempted Village Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Posting for a Teaching Position for the 2026-2027 School Year: POSITION: MS Intervention Specialist DISTRICT: Milton-Union Exempted Village Schools COUNTY: Miami DEADLINE: Until filled SALARY: Per the negotiated contract for Certificated Staff CONTACT: Justin Firks, MS Principal Milton-Union Middle School 7630 Milton-Potsdam Road West Milton, Ohio 45383 Phone: (937) 884-7930 Email: firksj@muschools.com JOB SUMMARY: Milton-Union Exempted Village Schools is looking for a middle school Intervention Specialist for the 2026-2027 school year. The instructor would primarily be responsible for serving our special education students in a co-teaching environment in the areas of Mathematics and Language Arts, with the opportunity to provide small group instruction during our Intervention and Enrichment class period. CERTIFICAITON/LICENSURE REQUIREMENTS: K-12 Intervention Specialist (Mild-Moderate) Milton-Union Exempted Village Schools are strongly committed to the use of technology in the classroom. Ideal candidates will have experience with related software, resources and applications that can be implemented in the classroom to improve the instructional process. The successful candidate may also be considered for various extra-curricular and/or co-curricular advising and coaching positions that may become available in the district. Job.JobDescription: School Psychologist JobID: 32955 Position Type: Student Services/ Psychologist Date Posted: 2/25/2026 Location: Montgomery County ESC Regional Center Date Available: ASAP District: Montgomery County Educational Service Center Additional Information: Show/Hide Montgomery County Educational Service Center 200 South Keowee Street, Dayton, OH 45402 (937) 225-4598 Job Title: Psychologist Job Location: Montgomery County ESC - Regional Center Start Date: ASAP Salary: Commensurate with experience Full/Part-Time: Full-Time Days: 185 Who We Are: We're a premier provider of high quality, cost effective, special education services in Ohio. We serve 16 public school districts in our county and more than 130 educational entities across Ohio. #WeAreMCESC! What We Offer: A unique opportunity to make an impact across multiple school communities through innovative, high-leverage consultation. A supportive, forward-thinking team environment with a national reputation for excellence. Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits. Continuous professional learning and leadership opportunities. What You'll Do: We are seeking a highly collaborative and knowledgeable School Psychologist with a passion for supporting mental wellness and positive behavior supports. This position is designed for professionals who have experience with removing barriers to learning through systems-level consultation, behavior intervention support, and the promotion of mental wellness and positive behavior supports. While some assessment is required, the primary focus of this role is consultation with district staff, administrators, and familiesâparticularly around challenging behavior, neuro-informed practices, restorative approaches, Positive Behavioral Interventions and Supports (PBIS), and building sustainable Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MTSS). Lead and support consultation efforts on challenging behaviors, partnering with educators, administrators, and families to develop proactive, trauma-informed strategies. Conduct neuro-informed Functional Behavior Assessments (FBAs) and develop comprehensive, self-regulation-focused Behavior Intervention Plans (BIPs). Provide training and consultation around PBIS/MTSS frameworks and implementation across school tiers. Support schools in fostering environments that promote self-regulation and co-regulation. Collaborate with districts and early childhood programs to design and refine MTSS systems that are inclusive and responsive to student needs. Ensure practices align with special education law, IDEA compliance, and evidence-based approaches. Deliver high-quality professional development and coaching to school staff and leadership. Become a member of a team known for innovative, cutting edge service delivery and national-level recognition. What You'll Bring: Valid School Psychologist license or certification in Ohio. Demonstrated expertise in behavioral consultation, FBAs, BIPs, PBIS, and trauma-informed, restorative practices. Strong knowledge of special education law and its practical application. Experience supporting MTSS frameworks and conducting systems-level work. Skilled in facilitating meaningful collaboration between educators, administrators, and families. Strong communication skills and a collaborative, solutions-oriented mindset. Commitment to high-performance standards, professional ethics, and student advocacy. Satisfactory fingerprinting and criminal records check results (provided for free by MCESC!). What's Next?: Please submit your application, letter of interest, and resume via the DASEC Frontline application portal: https://www.applitrack.com/dayton/onlineapp/ Connect with Us!: Website: https://www.mcesc.org/ LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/mcescohio/ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MCESCohio YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@MCESC/featured Montgomery County ESC provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassmen

Job.JobDescription: The Louisville Leopards are a D-III Girls Volleyball program. We are looking for a candidate that has had previous experience working at the varsity level, in an inter-scholastic coaching environment. The candidate should be familiar with local and state regulations and eligibility standards. Candidates must demonstrate the ability to develop a strong, cohesive program and be able to cultivate a culture focused on academics, athletic excellence and moral character. Qualified candidates shall possess effective communication skills, including the ability to establish rapport with parents, student athletes, administration, faculty and staff. They should also possess effective organizational skills, be committed to being a positive role model on and off the court, and have the knowledge to grow an overall successful volleyball program from youth to varsity level. Candidates should hold or be willing to obtain all necessary certifications as required by the state and district.

Job.JobTitle: Coaching - HS Girls Varsity Asst Volleyball Coach
Job.JobDescription: JOB RESPONSIBILITIES: Assist with all aspects of the Girls Volleyball Program including player development, game strategies, and team leadership. Assist with tryouts, practices, and games. Is knowledgeable about the game, particularly game management, and rules. Assist with the development and execution of an effective training program. Motivate and mentor student-athletes on and off the Court. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guide student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, discipline, respect, sportsmanship, and skills development. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Cooperate, in a timely manner, with School Athletic Director and the District Athletic Department in regard to requests for information that might be pertinent and/or periodically required. Meet all the requirements mandated by the District Board of Education, The Ohio High School Athletic Association (OHSAA), and The Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to the mandated Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/ Certification and the ODE Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Check the status of academic eligibility for all prospective student-athletes. Follow the District Athletic Department Policies as outlined in the Coaches Handbook and meet all criteria as outlined in the job description. Model the behavior expected of our student-athletes including timeliness to practices and games, being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Maintain a suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Accept and profit from constructive criticism and suggestions for improving coaching performance. Recognize the need to be aware of and implement all applicable administrative polices set forth by the District Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, ODE, and OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season conduct to student-athletes prior to the start of the season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Participate in Clinics and other available programs to improve coaching performance. Participate in School events (i.e., Awards Night, Pep Assemblies, Athletic Boosters Meetings, etc. Assist in ensuring that team is well-prepared for opponents. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies. SKILLS/QUALIFICATIONS: Fundraising experience. Strong knowledge of volleyball rules, techniques, and strategies. Must be passionate and dedicated to inspire student-athletes. Previous experience coaching volleyball preferred. Have the ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes. Serve as a positive role model. Demonstrated knowledge and skills associated with the sport. TRAINING/CERTIFICATIONS: Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Evidence of successful past coaching experience at the High School and/or College level preferred. Provide satisfactory result of County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and a FBI Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Provide proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification. CONTRACT PROVISIONS: TYPE OF CONTRACT: Supplemental Annual Limited SALARY: Per Schedule E Contract of current C.F.T. Labor Agreement REPORTS TO: School Principal APPLICATION PROCEDURE: Apply online via CPS Unified Talent SELECTION PROCEDURE: Interview
Job.CompanyDescription: Cincinnati City School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: Jenks Public Schools Job Description Position: Assistant Coach - Boys Volleyball Reports to: Director of Community Education and Assistant Director – Athletics, Community Education Supervises: None Qualifications: High School diploma or GED required. Has previous success in an assigned coaching position. Extensive knowledge, skills and ability in the strategy and technology of the sport. Experience interacting with student athletes, parents, and assistant coaches through deliberate positive interactions. Ability to serve as an effective role model for student athletes and represent Jenks Public Schools and Jenks Community Education with integrity and respect. Satisfy all district requirements with regard to physical health and citizenship. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable. Position Goal: Assist with all aspects of student-athlete development and performance of the volleyball program. Essential Functions and Responsibilities: Assist in the planning, development, and the execution of the high school boys volleyball program by focusing on building a positive culture and developing quality relationships with student athletes and parents. Assist in the implementation of sound practices and skill development that maximizes student athlete performance in volleyball in collaboration with the head coach. Communicate effectively with all program stakeholders to ensure a positive, quality experience is provided for our student athletes. Collaborate with the coordinator, assistant coordinator and the Booster Club. Assist in providing direction and evaluation of youth coaches and teams to develop vertical alignment throughout the program. Employment Terms: Community Education Classified - Temporary Contract FLSA Qualification: Non-Exempt Evaluation: None Physical Demands: This is an overview intended for compliance with the ADA – it is not an exhaustive list of the duties to be performed. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals to perform the essential functions of a specific position. Regular Requirements: Stand for extended periods; sit for extended periods; twist at the neck and trunk; bend at the waist; stoop, kneel, crouch; lift/move up to 20 pounds; handle objects and tools with hands; repetitive use of fingers and hands. Occasional Requirements: Reach with hands and arms overhead, above shoulder and horizontally; lift/move up to 75 pounds. Visual Requirements: Close, color, peripheral, depth perception, ability to adjust focus. Hearing/Speaking Requirements: Adequate to exchange information.

Head JVIII Volleyball Coach | Open Until Filled GENERAL DUTIES: Support the Hillsboro School District athletic objective, policy of interscholastic athletics, and Hillsboro School District Athletic Code. The District believes that extra-curricular activities are an integral part of school life. All students, regardless of their ability, should take part if possible to be able to grow physically, intellectually, and socially through participation. Coaches organize and conduct the athletic programs to meet the needs of the school district and their participants. ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS: Experience in and/or knowledge of the sport, coaching techniques, and student supervision. Ability and willingness to effectively communicate in verbal and written modes with student/athletes, parents, assistant coaches, and other school staff. Ability and willingness to perform coaching responsibilities in accordance with school and District procedures and policies. Must have current CPR/First Aid certification. Must have current Concussion Management and other trainings required by the OSAA. Maintain integrity of confidential information relating to students, staff, or District patrons. The ability to work harmoniously with others and collaborate solutions to concerns. Successfully pass background check. To view the full job description please visit the following link: Head Coach

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Head Swim Coach - Mountain View High School - Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application.
As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement.
Bend La Pine Schools Head Coaching Details
Applicant must submit: 1. Letter of application detailing experience. 2. Current Resume – listing experience related to this position and working with students. 3. A minimum of three letters of recommendation. 4. A minimum of three references with contact information.
General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Head Coach:
Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability academically and behaviorallyfor all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for program. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be readily accessible and visible to the athletes at all levels of the program. Other duties as assigned by Athletic Director.
Newly hired coaches will be required to complete or show proof of the following certifications prior to employment: 1. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching (one-time requirement, previous completion will be recognized) 2. Concussion in Sports (yearly) 3. Heat Illness Prevention (every 4 years) District Background check 4. Appearance and Performance Enhancing Drugs and Substances Training (every 4 years) 5. Heads up certification (Yearly) (Football only) 6. First Aid/CPR/AED (every 2 years) (paid coaches only) 7. Bend La Pine Safe Schools Trainings (Yearly)
Requirements Please Provide: Resume Cover Letter
Note: The system will not take uploads from current employees, so please email supporting documents to hr-recruitment@bend.k12.or.us
Additional Information School Year/Contract 2026/27 - Contract Days: 57 Posting End Date Apr 5, 2026 Benefit Status This position is not Benefited
Job.Location: Mountain View High School, 2755 NE 27th St, Bend, OR, 97701, United States

Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete. Recommend/assist in scheduling for your sport to the Athletic Director. Be readily accessible and visible to the athletes at all levels of the program. Other duties as assigned by Athletic Director.
Newly hired coaches will be required to complete or show proof of the following certifications prior to employment: 1. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching (one-time requirement, previous completion will be recognized) 2. Concussion in Sports (yearly) 3. Heat Illness Prevention (every 4 years) District Background check 4. Appearance and Performance Enhancing Drugs and Substances Training (every 4 years) 5. Heads up certification (Yearly) (Football only) 6. First Aid/CPR/AED (every 2 years) (paid coaches only) 7. Bend La Pine Safe Schools Trainings (Yearly)
Requirements Please Provide: Resume Cover Letter
Job.Location: Sky View Middle School, 63555 18th St, Bend, OR, 97701, United States

Job.JobDescription: Coach: Assistant Track - Riverside High School This position starts On Mach 1st 2026 . Riverside High School seeks a highly qualified individual to serve as an Assistant Track Coach with the expertise in throws (javelin, shot, discus). Riverside High School: Riverside is the newest comprehensive high school in the West Linn-Wilsonville School District. We are a school of choice for all students within the district and currently have 9th and 10th graders. We are a 3A OSAA Athletic Program. A successful candidate will possess the following qualities : Previous Coaching experience preferred. High ethical and professional standards. A thorough knowledge of the sport, skill development, and coaching techniques. The presence to be a role model for student athletes and the public face of the program. A commitment to the importance of academic excellence and achievement for all student-athletes in the program. Demonstrate ability to lead young athletes. A highly tuned ability to communicate effectively to a variety of stakeholders. Skill in organization, time management, and public relations. Employ good judgment in a variety of situations including emergencies. The Riverside Coach will be responsible for the following: -Recruit student-athletes into the program. -Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics. -Advocate for multi-sport athletes -Provide regular communication with parents/families. -Regularly track the academic process of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. -Work effectively and collaborate with other athletic programs to ensure off season/summer opportunities. -Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. -Hold all student athletes and coaching staff to high expectations. -Help develop the youth program below 9th grade level. -Work with local media as needed and establish strong working relationships with community support groups. Community and District Information : The West Linn-Wilsonville School District is nestled within two thriving cities, West Linn and Wilsonville, within the rural area of Clackamas County. Both communities have a strong legacy of support for their schools, music and arts endeavors, and the overall well-being of their patrons. The school district and surrounding communities have experienced steady, constant growth over the past three decades. The estimated student population of the District as of December 2024 is 8,900. It is the policy of the West Linn-Wilsonville Board of Education and School District (WLWV) that no discrimination or harassment on the grounds of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veterans' status, genetic information, or disability shall occur if the employee with or without reasonable accommodation, is able to perform the essential functions of the position. These protections apply to students, employees, and other members of the public. West Linn-Wilsonville School District is committed to equal access and equal opportunity in all activities/services, educational programs, and employment. Persons having questions regarding equal opportunity and nondiscrimination should contact the Director of Human Resources at the West Linn-Wilsonville School District, 503-673-7095.

e' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> INTERNAL ONLY - Behavioral Health Coach 2025-26 - MTSS/Social Emotional Learning JobID: 13520 Position Type: Classified - Department - Based Date Posted: 2/19/2026 Location: STRAUB MIDDLE SCHOOL (224) Closing Date: 03/07/2026 Additional Information: Show/Hide INTERNAL ONLY Click here for full job description: Behavioral Health Coach PURPOSE The Behavioral Health Coach enhances PreK12 learning environments by building districtwide capacity to support students with behavioral health and social-emotional needs. The position provides trauma-and stress-informed de-escalation training, develops and delivers curriculum for the Behavioral Health Coaching & Training Academy, and offers individualized coaching, classroom support, and consultation to educators and support staff. The role supports the implementation of behavior intervention strategies, assists with crisis response, contributes to multidisciplinary teams, and collaborates with Student Services, Safety and Risk Management, HR, and school-based staff to ensure safe, legally compliant, and culturally responsive practices. Through expert guidance, data-informed decision making, and a commitment to continuous improvement, the Behavioral Health Coach strengthens the district's capacity to create safe, inclusive, and supportive environments for all students and staff. *The Department of Behavioral Health is looking to hire a Behavioral Health Coach for our Coaching and Training Academy who is both technically proficient and relationally strong. We will prioritize candidates with expertise in trauma-informed practices, crisis intervention, and behavior systems, but who can also translate that knowledge into practical, coachable strategies for staff and students across diverse school environments. The ideal candidate would be calm and decisive under pressure, able to lead during high-stress situations while maintaining legal compliance and student-centered integrity. We are looking for professionals who build credibility through modeling, not just trainingsomeone who can step into classrooms, support teams, and elevate districtwide capacity through data-informed coaching. Finally, we want someone who aligns tightly with core valuesequity, relationships, and communitywhile holding high standards for safety, accountability, and continuous improvement. 2 Positions Type: Classified Hours per day: 7.5 Number of days per year: 260 Status: Permanent Starting July 1, 2026 Hourly Range: $34.05 - $43.49 Application Procedure: Apply online All Applicants, please note: If selected, you will be asked to participate in an interview process. Interviews are conducted virtually, in person, or via a one-way video. Please note interviews are a required part of the hiring process, non-compliance with the interview eliminates your eligibility for the position. Salem-Keizer Public Schools does not and shall not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, marital status, age, disability, veterans' status, or other categories protected by ORS Chapter 659A. Job.JobTitle: Head Girls Soccer Coach - Ida B. Wells-Barnett HS - 26/27 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice.
The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service.
504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660
Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process
DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions.
DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265.
Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities.
The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics.
All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings.
Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference.
All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records.
Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00
* There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts.

All students, regardless of their ability, should take part if possible to be able to grow physically, intellectually, and socially through participation. Coaches organize and conduct the athletic programs to meet the needs of the school district and their participants. ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS: Experience in and/or knowledge of the sport, coaching techniques, and student supervision. Ability and willingness to effectively communicate in verbal and written modes with student/athletes, parents, assistant coaches, and other school staff. Ability and willingness to perform coaching responsibilities in accordance with school and District procedures and policies. Must have current CPR/First Aid certification. Must have current Concussion Management and other trainings required by the OSAA. Maintain integrity of confidential information relating to students, staff, or District patrons. The ability to work harmoniously with others and collaborate solutions to concerns. Successfully pass background check. To view the full job description please visit the following link: Head Coach

ss='postingsList' id='p1162_'> Academic Intervention Specialist JobID: 1162 Position Type: High School Teaching Date Posted: 2/19/2026 Location: Reading HS Innovation Academy Additional Information: Show/Hide Position Title: Academic Intervention Specialist Classification: REA Reports To: Building Principal Position Goal: To assist with the implementation of interventions and professional development aspects of the academic component of the District’s and building’s comprehensive plan Qualifications: Level II instructional certificate preferred; Master’s Degree in Education or related field preferred Preferably a minimum of 10 years of successful/satisfactory teaching; otherwise qualified candidates may satisfy experience component through a Central Office assessment of demonstrated educational leadership Demonstrated desire to assume additional responsibility and pursue career advancement Demonstrated willingness to assume leadership positions Demonstrated knowledge of researched-based instructional strategies that engage all students Demonstrated thorough knowledge of elementary and/or secondary curriculum and subject matter Strong organizational skills with a proven ability to prioritize as appropriate Ability to communicate effectively, both in writing and orally Proven ability to effectively present to adults in a learning/coaching environment Ability to work effectively and cooperatively with administrative, professional, and non-professional staff, with a particular emphasis on building capacity amongst professional colleagues ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: The essential functions of this position include, but are not limited to the following fundamental duties: Provides support in analyzing student assessment data. Monitors intervention programs by providing support to the teachers. Implements interventions to students Models lessons when appropriate. Provides assistance in researching intervention materials. Provides encouragement and emotional support to teachers. Works positively toward meeting identified District and building improvement goals. Develops and maintains a confidential, collegial relationship with teachers. Assists building and classroom level educators in implementing challenging and rigorous curriculum based on the Pennsylvania Core Curriculum Content Standards Uses student work and assessment results as rationale and evidence to assists teachers in making decisions about instructional activities. Maintains all relative paperwork consistently and in an appropriate manner. Develops a schedule that is appropriate and equitable to teachers within the schools Participates in professional development activities to build capacity as a resource for schools and to model professional behavior. Prepares materials for use by the teachers, department, and school. Attend PDE initiated workshops. Work with administration to plan and facilitate PD for all staff members. Perform duties as assigned by the MTSS Coordinator and/or Chief Instructional Services Officer. TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Full-time (10-month) exempt position. Salary and benefits determined by the REA collective bargaining agreement. EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated in accordance with provisions of the Pennsylvania Public School Code of 1949, as amended The position holder must be able to perform the essential job functions with or without reasonable accommodation. It is the responsibility of the employee to inform the school district administrator designated as immediate supervisor of the position, of any and all reasonable accommodations that will be required. The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. ='postingsList' id='p1178_'> Assistant Principal, Middle School JobID: 1178 Position Type: RSAA Act 93 Administrative/Supervisory Positions/ Assistant Principal Date Posted: 3/2/2026 Location: Northwest Middle School Additional Information: Show/Hide Position Title: Assistant Principal Classification: RSAA Range: High School /Middle School at Range 5, Elementary at Range 6 Reports To: Building Principal Position Goal: The purpose of the position is to increase student achievement by engaging in the following: SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: Supervises all professional and support staff as assigned. QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE: Appropriate Master’s Degree required; Minimum five years teaching experience preferred. Demonstrated leadership ability and knowledge in instructional best practices is required. Experience teaching at the level of the position (i.e. Elementary teaching experience for Elementary level AP position; Secondary or Mid-Level teaching experience for Secondary or Mid-Level AP positions) is preferred but not required. Past experience with instructional coaching, design and delivery of professional development and building/district level committee involvement and experience working in an urban environment is strongly preferred. CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS: PA Teaching Certificate, PA Administrative Certification – K-12 Principal (or appropriate level K-6 or 7-12 Principal Certification) ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Other duties may be assigned. The essential functions of this position include, but are not limited to the following fundamental duties: Supervision of Teachers Possess a deep knowledge regarding best practices, current instructional programs, and content in order to make professional and quality judgment on instruction. Observe and evaluate professional staff accurately, consistently and based on evidence while aligning with the philosophy, mission and goals of the Reading School District. Data Manage, interpret and utilize data to organize differentiated groups and address all students’ individual academic needs in collaboration with the leadership team. Professional Support for Teachers Possess deep content knowledge in order to provide professionals and para professionals with strategies and activities that will support instruction in core curriculum. Monitor and facilitate high quality professional development through turn around training while working with staff (in teams or individually) to refine knowledge and skills, based on district and school data. Provide small group or individual support as needed following professional development. Identify school teaching and learning needs as well as barriers and weaknesses by analyzing students’ data and implementing problem-solving actions with teachers. Demonstrate instructional leadership to facilitate meetings that focus on student achievement. Instruct teachers in using data for instructional planning that will have a positive impact on student achievement. Professionalism Participate in professional development that enhances knowledge of instructional practice, delivery and student achievement. Establish a professional rapport with staff built on mutual respect. Display the highest ethical and professional behavior and standards when working as serving as a role model to teachers. Use effective interpersonal skills to communicate professionally with staff, students and parents that lead to positive relationships. Performs other duties as assigned by the Superintendent or his/her designee. This job description does not constitute an exhaustive list of responsibilities and management may revise it at any

Job.JobTitle: Head Coach Unified Track and Field
Job.JobDescription: ='postingsList' id='p2686_'> Head Coach Unified Track and Field JobID: 2686 Position Type: Extracurricular/ Extracurricular Date Posted: 1/30/2026 Location: Hempfield High School Additional Information: Show/Hide Unified Track and Field Team Head Coach Hempfield High School Job Summary: Oversee the administration of the high school's Unified Track and Field team which is composed of a proportional number of students who are Special Olympics-eligible (defined as students with an intellectual disability, cognitive delay, or developmental disability) and students without intellectual disabilities. Position Information: Part-time, no benefits 2025-2026 Salary - $600 2 practices per week, 1 home meet, and 2 away meets with an estimated 40 hours for the season Roles & Responsibilities: Liaison regularly with the school's athletic director and Special Olympics Pennsylvania to ensure quality program standards are being met. Develop and implement practice plans in which track and field instruction and coaching is provided to all participants. Impart the philosophy of a positive inclusive sports experience by following the principles of Unified Sports®. Provide guidance and manage team strategies at Unified Track and Field competitions. Recruit a proportional number of students who are Special Olympics-eligible. Handle any paperwork or administrative duties required by the school district and/or Special Olympics Pennsylvania. If serving as a host site for a Unified Track and Field competition, recruit volunteers to help conduct and manage the events. Attend the free Unified Sports® Track and Field Coaches' Training in your county. Complete the free online Special Olympics coaches' courses. Qualifications: Coaching experience preferred Evidence of effective leadership Ability to articulate vision for building/enhancing the scholastic program Excellent communication and problem solving skills Required Documents: Submission of a pre-employment medical examination Current PA Criminal History background check Current Federal Criminal background check Current Child Abuse background check Interested candidates should submit an online application. Questions can be directed to hroffice@hempfieldsd.org . EOE Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Assistant Coach-Unified Track and Field

Job Details Title: Assistant Coach - Boys Volleyball Location: High School Available: ASAP Reports to: Athletic Director Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a motivated and knowledgeable individual to serve as an Assistant Coach for our Boys Volleyball program. This role supports the Head Coach in providing leadership, mentorship, and instruction to student-athletes while promoting a positive, inclusive team culture. Key Responsibilities: Assist the Head Coach with planning and leading practices and competitions. Support the development of student-athletes’ skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Promote academic achievement and personal growth among athletes. Assist with supervision and safety of all team members during activities. Help manage equipment and maintain team standards of conduct. Model positive behavior and represent the school in a professional manner. Follow all school, district, league, and state athletic association rules. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent. Some coaching or playing experience in the sport (preferably at the high school level). Basic understanding of the game, rules, and strategy. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with the Head Coach and school staff. Preferred Qualifications: CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience working with youth or student-athletes. Familiarity with school athletic program policies and procedures. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements : Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB test within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates applying for the above position should submit an online application. JOB GOAL Supporting students in the school building as well as the community setting as directed and supervised by the Transition Coordinator or Co-Op Coordinator. Assist in production and processing of classroom materials and projects as related to transitional skills. 9. Maintain positive rapport with community employers and volunteer sites, as well as communicate schedule changes, concerns and progress with transition coordinator or co-op coordinator. Job.JobDescription: EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER 11.11.25 Cheltenham School District announces the following extra duty-extra pay position(s) is available for the 2025-2026 school-year, at Cedarbrook Middle School: Tennis [Boys] - Head Coach Must have extensive knowledge of the sport and the ability to effectively teach and communicate fundamental skills. Job.JobDescription: Band Instructor (Pit) JobID: 1602 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 1/9/2026 Location: Erie High School Additional Information: Show/Hide Position Title: Band (Pit) Instructor Location: TBD Position Summary/Goal: To provide an educational atmosphere in which students will move toward the fulfillment of their potential for intellectual, emotional, physical and psychological growth. Term of Employment: 12 Month Position Reporting Relationships: Reports to Marching Band Director/Athletic Director Qualifications: Possess the ability to effectively advise and instruct middle through high school aged students in the skills of pit percussion instruments. Possess the ability to instruct percussion instruments with proficiency including, but not limited to the following: xylophone, marimba, bells, vibraphone, chimes, and auxiliary percussion. Have the knowledge and ability to manage and instruct the use of a sound board and system as well as electronics such as synthesizers, sample pad, etc. Possess the ability to work with band director, and other members of marching band staff to cooperate in the designing of competitive marching band program. Have a willingness to attend all rehearsals and functions as scheduled by the marching band director. Have the ability to identify and coach students of various developmental levels in order to insure individual success as performers. Demonstrate experience in competitive marching band (minimum 3 years). Collaborate with music instructors. Requirements: Strong communication, public relations, and interpersonal skills Must hold or obtain FBI, Criminal Check, and Child Abuse Clearances. Evaluation: Annual evaluation will be conducted by the Dean of VPA with input from the Marching Band Director. This list is intended to be illustrative rather than complete and serves to show major duties and responsibilities and does not express or imply that these are the only duties to be performed by the incumbent in this position. The employee will be required to perform any other position-related duties requested by the supervisorc. THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE IN EMPLOYMENT, EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS OR ACTIVITIES BASED ON AGE, RACE, COLOR, RELIGION, NATIONAL ORIGIN, SEX, DISABILITY OR BECAUSE A PERSON IS A DISABLED VETERAN OR VETERAN OF THE VIETNAM ERA. THIS POLICY OF NON-DISCRIMINATION EXTENDS TO ALL OTHER LEGALLY PROTECTED CLASSIFICATIONS. PUBLICATION OF THIS POLICY IS IN ACCORDANCE WITH STATE AND FEDERAL LAWS INCLUDING TITLE VII OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964, TITLE IX OF THE EDUCATION AMENDMENTS OF 1972, SECTION 504 OF THE REHABILITATION ACT OF 1973, AND THE AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT. INQUIRIES MAY BE MADE BY CONTACTING THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT, 148 WEST 21ST STREET, ERIE PA 16502, (814) 874-6080. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 Football Equipment Manager

Job.JobDescription: ul class='postingsList' id='p1613_'> Football Equipment Manager JobID: 1613 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 3/3/2026 Location: School Location (to be determined) Additional Information: Show/Hide Football Equipment Manager High School and Middle School Positions Available: (1) Position Summary The Football Equipment Manager is responsible for the management, distribution, maintenance, inventory control, and safety compliance of all football equipment for the Erie School District Football Program (Varsity, Junior Varsity, and Middle School). This position plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with PIAA, NFHS, PDE, and NOCSAE regulations and supporting athlete safety standards consistent with a PIAA program. Term of Employment 12-Month Position Location Erie High School Reporting Relationships Reports to the Head Football Coach Reports to the Director of Athletics Collaborates with Athletic Trainer and Building Administration Qualifications Experience managing athletic equipment, preferably football. Knowledge of PIAA, NFHS, and NOCSAE equipment standards. Understanding of proper helmet and shoulder pad fitting protocols. Strong organizational, inventory management, and documentation skills. Ability to lift, transport, and manage heavy equipment. Strong interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to work evenings, weekends, and extended seasonal hours. Urban student experience preferred. Required Clearances & Certifications Act 34  PA Criminal History Clearance Act 151  Child Abuse Clearance Act 114  FBI Fingerprint Clearance Mandated Reporter Training Concussion Awareness Certification and Sudden Cardiac (NFHS) Completion of all required district compliance trainings Essential Duties and Responsibilities Safety & Compliance Ensure all helmets and protective equipment meet NOCSAE standards and are annually reconditioned and certified. Maintain documentation of serial numbers, reconditioning dates, and compliance records. Remove damaged or non-compliant equipment from circulation immediately. Ensure adherence to all PIAA and NFHS equipment regulations. Support district concussion and emergency action protocols. Inventory & Equipment Operations Maintain a comprehensive inventory system for Varsity, JV, and Middle School football equipment. Supervise distribution, fitting, tracking, and collection of all equipment and uniforms. Conduct pre-season and post-season equipment audits. Maintain secure and organized equipment storage areas. Coordinate ordering and replacement of equipment in collaboration with the Head Coach and Athletic Director. Adhere to district purchasing and budget procedures. Game & Practice Responsibilities Attend all home football games (MS, JV, Varsity). Attend designated practices to manage equipment needs. Coordinate transport, setup, and breakdown of equipment for home and away contests. Off-Season Responsibilities Conduct post-season inventory and damage assessments. Coordinate helmet and pad reconditioning process. Develop equipment replacement plan aligned with safety standards. Participate in planning meetings with the Athletic Department. Professional Expectations Demonstrate professionalism and ethical conduct at all times. Maintain positive working relationships with coaches, student-athletes, and staff. Represent Erie's Public Schools in a professional and responsible manner. Evaluation Performance will be evaluated by the Head Football Coach and Director of Athletics in accordance with district policies and the Athletic Handbook. This description is intended to be illustrative rather than exhaustive. Additional duties may be assigned as needed. THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE IN EMPLOYMENT, EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS OR ACTIVITIES BASED ON AGE, RACE, COLOR, RELIGION, NATIONAL ORIGIN, SEX, DISABILITY OR BECAUSE A PERSON IS A DISABLED VETERAN OR VETERAN OF THE VIETNAM ERA. THIS POLICY OF NON-DISCRIMINATION EXTENDS TO ALL OTHER LEGALLY PROTECTED CLASSIFI
