Job.JobTitle: Head Coach Boys Junior Varsity Baseball
Job.JobDescription: ingsList' id='p1614_'> Head Coach Boys Junior Varsity Baseball JobID: 1614 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 3/3/2026 Location: Erie High School Additional Information: Show/Hide Head Coach Boys Junior Varsity Baseball Position Summary/Goal: The Head Coach will facilitate and enhance communication and collaboration between and among the Erie School District and parents, students, and community centers. The Head Coach will promote a positive school culture and foster academic excellence and exemplify good character for the individual athlete. Furthermore, the Erie School District expects the Head Coach to be a positive role model, a mentor to student-athletes, and embrace all concepts of good citizenship. Term of Employment: 12 month position, effective for the 2025-2026 School Year Schools/Location: Erie High School Reporting Relationships: High School Varsity Head Coach Reports to Director of Athletics May work on specific projects with program directors or building principals. Qualifications: Head coaching experience at the high school or middle school level, minimum of 3 years experience required. Evidence of leading a successful middle school or high school baseball program with a winning record and post season coaching experience. Ability to transition student athletes to the next competitive level (ability to articulate concrete examples and procedures regarding the transition of student athletes to the next competitive level). The ability to design offensive and defensive strategies. Experience in coaching offense and defense. Evidence of practice plans developed in prior coaching positions. Must hold or obtain FBI, Criminal Check, and Child Abuse Clearances. Working knowledge of Erie School District policies, procedures, and organizational structure. Knowledge of current trends, tactics, strategies and skills in baseball. Must possess strong interpersonal skills and communication skills. Ability to build positive relationships with urban student populations. Urban student experience a plus. Requirements: Must complete all Coaching Education requirements of the PIAA and PDE in a timely manner. Must adhere to all PIAA bylaws and eligibility standards. Must hold or obtain Act 34 (Criminal History), Act 151 (Child Abuse), and Act 114 (FBI Fingerprint) clearances. Completion of mandated reporter training. Completion of concussion management certification and other state required safety trainings. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Instruct student-athletes in the fundamentals of baseball, including rules of the game, proper techniques, and skill development. Plan and carry out daily practice plans aligned with the varsity program philosophy. Supervise all practices, games, and team-related activities. Assist varsity coach with development of a structured feeder system throughout the baseball program. Promote, maintain, and ensure high academic standards for all athletes. Monitor academic progress and secure support when needed. Conduct pre-season and post-season conditioning programs in collaboration with district guidelines. Promote good sportsmanship and serve as a positive role model. Maintain positive communication with athletes, families, community members, and district administration. Ensure compliance with all PIAA, district, and state regulations. Develop and clearly communicate expectations for athlete participation and conduct. Assist with scheduling in coordination with the Athletic Director and Varsity Coach. Manage ordering, collection, distribution, and inventory of equipment and uniforms. Submit required paperwork, eligibility forms, and roster information in a timely manner. Maintain accurate records including attendance, statistics, and equipment logs. Professionalism: Must possess the ability to be a collaborative team player. Must be cooperative, courteous, service-oriented, and promote these characteristics within the team. Job.JobDescription: Job Details Title: Assistant Coach - Unified Track & Field Location: Available: ASAP Reports to: Athletic Director Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a motivated and knowledgeable individual to serve as an Assistant Coach for our Unified Track & Field program. This role supports the Head Coach in providing leadership, mentorship, and instruction to student-athletes while promoting a positive, inclusive team culture. Key Responsibilities: Assist the Head Coach with planning and leading practices and competitions. Support the development of student-athletes’ skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Promote academic achievement and personal growth among athletes. Assist with supervision and safety of all team members during activities. Help manage equipment and maintain team standards of conduct. Model positive behavior and represent the school in a professional manner. Follow all school, district, league, and state athletic association rules. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent. Some coaching or playing experience in the sport (preferably at the high school level). Basic understanding of the game, rules, and strategy. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with the Head Coach and school staff. Preferred Qualifications: CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience working with youth or student-athletes. Familiarity with school athletic program policies and procedures. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements : Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB test within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates applying for the above position should submit an online application. EOE

Job Details Title: Head Coach - Unified Track & Field Reports to: Athletic Director Location: High School Available: ASAP Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR Contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement. Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a dedicated, experienced, and student-centered individual to serve as the Head Coach for our Unified Track & Field program. This position plays a critical role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field, fostering a culture of excellence, sportsmanship, and teamwork. Key Responsibilities: Lead and manage all aspects of the [sport name] program at the high school level. Plan, organize, and supervise practices and competitions. Ensure the physical and emotional safety of all student-athletes. Promote academic achievement, character development, and personal responsibility. Supervise assistant coaches and collaborate with athletic department staff. Maintain and inventory equipment and uniforms. Represent the program positively within the school and the broader community. Comply with school, district, league, and state athletic association policies and regulations. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor’s degree preferred). Prior coaching experience, preferably at the high school level. Knowledge of rules, strategies, and techniques of the sport. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to build positive relationships with students, parents, and staff. Preferred Qualifications: Valid teaching certificate (if applicable or required by district). CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience managing budgets, schedules, and program development. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements : Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB must be dated within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates must complete an online application. EOE

Job.JobTitle: DHMS - Extra Duty Position - Boys' Track Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription: Extra Pay for Extra Duty Position - Drexel Hill Middle School - Boys' Track Assistant Coach Upper Darby School District is seeking qualified seasonal Coach candidates for the position listed above. Successful candidates will have knowledge of the sport and will demonstrate effective coaching technique, leadership and mentoring skills; the ability to relate well within the school community; and the desire to provide an atmosphere conducive to good sportsmanship and high standards. Qualified candidates will enhance the School's commitment to diversity and inclusivity and will be committed to the School's Vision and Mission. Qualifications Prior successful coaching experience, preferably at the high school and/or college level Excellent communication, organizational, and interpersonal skills A strong character with a team-oriented spirit, and the ability to connect meaningfully with students throughout our diverse community Considerable knowledge of and experience coaching that sport, including competitions, practices, and strength/conditioning Effective verbal and written communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to organize and prioritize work High school or collegiate playing experience Experience working with middle and high school-aged students A positive student-centered approach to coaching Demonstrated technical, theoretical, and applied knowledge pertinent to the assigned sport CPR and first aid certified or the ability to become certified. Strong affinity for the mission of Upper Darby School District and an educational philosophy consistent with the school's mission and values Knowledge of basic computer operations Required Clearances include Criminal Background Clearance (Act 34), Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151), and FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) Meets criteria for employment in a PA Public School The mental and physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions Ability to sit and stand for extended periods of time Ability to bend, stoop, climb stairs, and reach overhead Ability to occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds Noise level in the work environment is usually moderate to loud depending on the specific site Responsibilities and Essential Functions Reports To: Director of Athletics Successful candidates will have knowledge of the sport and will demonstrate effective coaching technique, leadership and mentoring skills; the ability to relate well within the school community; and the desire to provide an atmosphere conducive to good sportsmanship and high standards Qualified candidates will enhance the School's commitment to diversity and inclusivity and will be committed to the School's Vision and Mission This list is intended to be illustrative rather than complete and serves to show major duties and responsibilities and does not express or imply that these are the only duties to be The Coach will be required to perform other duties requested by the Director of Athletics and/or school administrator Observe the rules of the PIAA, PIAA District 1, and the Central Athletic League (CAL) Provide leadership and general team management in the areas of daily practice, skills, and character development Remain current with theories and techniques in their sport Attend all coaches' meetings and collaborate with assistant coach(es) and student manager Establish and maintain effective working relationships with coaches, athletic administrators, students, parent body, and the community Evaluate and assess players' skills and select team rosters Responsible for team equipment Keep the scorebook current and available Report areas of concern immediately to Athletic Directors - fields, transportation, facilities, opposing teams, student behavior, parent issues, etc Initiate appropriate and regular communication with student-athletes and parents Follow school procedures regarding transportation, injuries, participation, meals Report and follow up with athletic trainer about injuries Write and submit individual comments on each student as scheduled Manage athletes at the appropriate level of the assigned sport Monitor student-athlete academic progress and academic advisement Enforces the CCS Student Code of Character Promotes good citizenship through actions as a role model Conducts himself or herself according to professional, ethical principles that reflect favorably upon the individual and the school Maintains the highest level of confidentiality pertaining to school and students' information, progress, and records Abides by the CCS Employee Manual and all current PA Public School expectations Our Mission: It is the mission of the Upper Darby School District to provide a comprehensive educational program that develops all learners into critical thinkers, with effective communication skills, empowering them to adapt to an ever-changing world. Our students will demonstrate integrity and confidence while cultivating a learner's mindset in pursuit of personal excellence and service to others. Our Belief Statements: Upper Darby School District's academic programs are based on the following beliefs: We believe: All individuals are capable of learning. Individuals learn in different ways and at different rates. Individuals have the right to be treated with respect and dignity. Students are unique individuals with varied talents and skills. Successful learning experiences build self-esteem. Learning is most effective in a caring environment where high standards of social interaction are maintained. Education should encourage individuals to acquire knowledge and attitudes necessary to live in a changing global society. Critical thinking, problem solving and decision-making are essential life skills. Updates in technology necessitate continuous personal and organizational adjustment. Professional development of all staff is vital to meet the changing needs of students and to reflect current best practices. The educational program must meet the needs of all learners and be responsive to the needs and resources of the community. Cultural and ethnic diversity is a valued strength of our community. Society benefits when individual rights are balanced with the needs of the group as a whole. Quality public education directly benefits the entire community. Lifelong learning is essential for all individuals. Education is the responsibility of the entire community, the school, the family, and, most importantly, the individual. The Upper Darby School District is an equal opportunity education institution and does not discriminate in its educational programs, activities, or employment practices on the basis of race, color, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion, ancestry, union membership, or any other legally protected category. Announcement of this policy is in accordance with state law, including the Pennsylvania Human Relations Act, and with federal law, including Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, and the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990. INTERESTED APPLICANTS SHOULD APPLY BY VISITING www.upperdarbysd.org > Services>Human Resources>Employment Opportunities

Job.JobDescription: Job Details Title: Assistant Coach - Flag Football Location: High School Available: ASAP Reports to: Athletic Director Terms of Employment: Stipend in accordance with EDR contract as stated in the PEA Collective Bargaining Agreement Description: Pennridge School District is seeking a motivated and knowledgeable individual to serve as an Assistant Coach for our Flag Football program. This role supports the Head Coach in providing leadership, mentorship, and instruction to student-athletes while promoting a positive, inclusive team culture. Key Responsibilities: Assist the Head Coach with planning and leading practices and competitions. Support the development of student-athletes’ skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Promote academic achievement and personal growth among athletes. Assist with supervision and safety of all team members during activities. Help manage equipment and maintain team standards of conduct. Model positive behavior and represent the school in a professional manner. Follow all school, district, league, and state athletic association rules. Minimum Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent. Some coaching or playing experience in the sport (preferably at the high school level). Basic understanding of the game, rules, and strategy. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with the Head Coach and school staff. Preferred Qualifications: CPR/First Aid certification. NFHS coaching certification or willingness to obtain. Experience working with youth or student-athletes. Familiarity with school athletic program policies and procedures. Pre-Employment Paperwork Requirements : Physical and Clearances must be dated within the last 12-months. TB test within the last 90-days. PA State Police Clearance (Act 34) Child Abuse Clearance (Act 151) FBI Fingerprint Clearance (Act 114) School Personnel Health Form (Physical and TB test) PDE 6004 Form (Act 24) Sexual Misconduct/Abuse Disclosure Release Form(s) (Act 168) Procedure: Interested candidates applying for the above position should submit an online application. EOE

Job.JobDescription: Self-directed professional with the knowledge and experience to work effectively in the field with learning support teachers, assess program quality and provide feedback to supervisors in order to sustain and develop high quality programs and practices. Design and implementation of staff-development sessions. Support teachers in program planning, IEP development, technology application and classroom management. Essential Duties and Responsibilities (List is not all-inclusive): 1. Teacher Support Coaching and Modeling of current instructional, social/emotional programs and lesson plan design with intensive support for new teachers Developing a framework for organization and management of the learning support classroom including effective use of para professionals Problem solving to meet the needs of the multiple learning profiles and social/emotional weaknesses of students within the present-day learning support programs Supporting the IEP process to include the development, implementation and progress monitoring of individualized educational programs Support technology implementation and application in order to support instructional program planning and participation in teaching-learning process. 2. Program Development Staying current in research based best practices for all students serviced in the learning support program Assessing and responding to needs within the instructional program Spearhead multi-year instructional initiatives Monitoring the effectiveness of current programs through data collection and analysis Coordinating and utilizing in district and out of district resources 3. Staff Development Provide staff development to special education teachers and para professionals based on Supervisor direction Provide training, support and feedback to teachers with Orton-Gillingham methodology and instructional practices Generate and provide staff development in programs and protocols that are standard to the general education and special education settings.
Job.SalaryRange: Salary in accordance with CBEA Contract

Job.JobDescription: Cranston Public Sch. is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Under direction of the school principal, Head Coach and the Athletic Director/or appropriate supervisor/Administrator, Individuals will be available three hours each day participate at games, and scout opponents whenever assigned to this task by the Head Coach. This position is contingent upon the following: Preference will be given to internal/school based staff and co-op school staff. Successful recruitment of team members and ability to field a team for practice and game conditions. You must complete the new hire compliance training prior to your first day of employment Bilingual Preferred Selected candidate will be required to pass a pre-employment criminal history background check. Selected candidates cannot begin work until passing their background check. Proper physical conditioning related to the sport during practice sessions. Successful completion of the interscholastic season Salary Rate Range: 8% of Teacher Salary Scale: Track, Outdoor Track, Boys/Girls Basketball, Softball, Soccer, Softball, Volleyball 10% of Teacher Salary Scale: Football EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: Must be certified in accordance with the new RIIL Coaching Permit Requirements DESCRIPTION OF DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Follow instructions issued by the Head Coach. Assist in the issuing, maintaining, and collecting of equipment. Perform duties in accordance with the Providence School Department Athletic Handbook. Teach fundamentals and skills. Teach rules and regulations. Display good sportsmanship at all times. Supervise players at all times. Assume responsibility for related duties that may be assigned by the Head Coach.

Under direction of the school principal at Classical High School and the Athletic Director/or appropriate supervisor/ Administrator, Individuals will be available three hours each day during the entire season. This position is contingent upon the following: Preference will be given to internal/school based staff and co-op school staff. Successful recruitment of team members and ability to field a team for practice and game conditions. Proper physical conditioning related to the sport during practice sessions. Successful completion of the interscholastic season You must complete the new hire compliance training prior to your first day of employment Bilingual Preferred Selected candidate will be required to pass a pre-employment criminal history background check. Selected candidates cannot begin work until passing their background check. Salary Rate Range: 10% of Teacher Salary Scale Note that for Competitive Cheer, the 10% stipend will be paid only during the Winter sports season. EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: RIDE Requirements: Must be certified in accordance with the new RIIL Coaching Permit Requirements Qualifications: BA in education or related field preferred but not required. Have current CPR/First Aid Certification in possession (required) Principles of Coaching. Psychology of Coaching. Athletic Training (Prevention and care of athletic injuries). Bio-mechanics (Analysis and understanding of human movement). Previous playing and coaching experience preferred. Dedication to the service of youth and ability to gain and hold the respect of team members. DESCRIPTION OF DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Teach fundamentals and skills. Organize practice sessions. Keep records and report results to local media. Provide Principal and Athletic Director an outline of a general practice session. Conduct coaches meetings and assign coaches responsibilities. Provide for care and prevention of injuries. Conform to all rules established by the Principal's Committee on Athletics and perform duties in accordance with the Providence School Department Athletic Handbook. Teach rules and regulations of the sport. Display good sportsmanship at all times. Responsible for the issuing, maintaining, and collecting of equipment. Prepare team budget. Recruitment of players and advancement of the sport.

Summary: Athletic positions require someone with proven decision-making skills, a track record of initiative and effort and a commitment to young people and education. Our jobs require a “people person” that indicates through actions a personality that is conducive to positive interactions with students, staff and parents.
To view the job description for each type of athletic coach, click here on the Athletics Position Descriptions. Click here on the Supplements for Athletics link to see athletics supplementary salary schedule.
Coaching positions require the following Continuing Education Requirements: - GCS Safety Videos Required Annually: - Bloodborne Pathogen Exposure Prevention - FERPA: Confidentiality of Records - GCS – Title IX & Safe Schools - GCS – Code of Ethics & Social Media - GCS – Information Security Mgmt System - GCS – Safety Training - GCS – Mandated Reporter - Greenville Energy Conservation - Hazard Communication: Right to Understand - Health Emergencies: Overview - Sexual Harassment: Staff to Staff - GCS – Active Shooter Training - NFHS Courses Required Annually: - Concussion in Sport - Heat Illness Prevention - Sudden Cardiac Arrest - Protecting Students from Abuse - NFHS Accredited Interscholastic Coach – Required Once for all New Head Coaches - Fundamentals of Coaching ($60) - Football Coaches - Heads UP Football Safety Coach Certification - Strength Coaches - NHSSCA – Must be certified to receive the strength supplement - Training Required in 1st Year then Every 2 Years: CPR – The GCS CPR certification process is offered in a blended learning format, which means you complete an online course FIRST, and then follow it with a hands-on skills evaluation session within a maximum of 60 days after online course completion.

Job.JobTitle: 2026-2027 LEAP Alternative Pathways to Teaching Pool
Job.JobDescription: 'p17321_'> 2026-2027 LEAP Alternative Pathways to Teaching Pool JobID: 17321 Position Type: Alternative Pathway to Certification/ LEAP Date Posted: 10/3/2025 Location: To Be Determined Additional Information: Show/Hide Description : Beaufort County School District's LEAP program offers job-embedded professional development to obtain teacher certification. You will work side-by-side with experts from the district through coaching and mentoring in your classroom, all while earning a teacher's salary and benefits ! View the full flyer HERE . For redirection to the LEAP webpage, click HERE . Qualifications: Minimum of a bachelor's degree Minimum GPA of 2.5 For secondary certification: 30 credit hours in related coursework Content-area Praxis exam Make sure to upload your transcript(s) as part of your application. Certification Areas: Early Childhood Elementary Special Education English Language Arts Math Social Studies Science Art Instrumental Music Chinese Spanish Cost: $2,250 total cost Deducted from biweekly paycheck over two years This job posting is not for a school specific position. The intent of this posting is to allow candidates to submit their application as a way of indicating that they are interested in a teaching position for the current or next school year. This will allow Human Resources to contact candidates about possible opportunities within our district. As school specific jobs are identified, applicants will need to apply for each position that they are interested in pursuing. For questions, please contact our LEAP team at bcsdLEAP@beaufort.k12.sc.us Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/5/2026 2026-2027 Athletic Director

ul class='postingsList' id='p17442_'> Assistant Football Coach JobID: 17442 Position Type: Athletics / Activities/ ATHLETIC COACH Date Posted: 11/21/2025 Location: MCCRACKEN MIDDLE Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION PURPOSE: Prepares each participating student athlete for competition in sports activities, and helps them to achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. Teaching certificate and experience preferred. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and

> Head Girls Soccer Coach JobID: 17508 Position Type: Athletics / Activities/ ATHLETIC COACH Date Posted: 12/31/2025 Location: LADYS ISLAND MIDDLE Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION PURPOSE: Prepares each participating student athlete for competition in sports activities, and helps them to achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. Teaching certificate and experience preferred. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordanc

Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. Teaching certificate and experience preferred. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. 'postingsList' id='p17764_'> 2026-2027 Academic Interventionist JobID: 17764 Position Type: Certified - Elementary School Date Posted: 3/2/2026 Location: WHALE BRANCH ELEMENTARY (High-Needs School) Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION PURPOSE: Responsible for supporting the school-wide literacy/math programs and for providing instruction to students in small and large group settings. The Interventionist uses research and best practices in the teaching of reading, writing, listening, and speaking across the content areas and works directly with students to improve their literacy levels. The Interventionist works with colleagues to identify students who are in need of instructional support. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Uses research-based instruction and data to develop instructional support and progress monitoring plan for students. Provides research based reading interventions(MTSS) to students who need support. Attends the ongoing professional development provided by the school and district. Attends required meetings in current research and practices. Uses student assessment data to guide instruction. Plans student interventions with teachers and Instructional Coaches. Provides intervention and support directly to students reading below grade level. Uses best and effective practices in daily instruction of students. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Must have a valid South Carolina teaching or be able to obtain one within 60 days from being hired. Must be Highly Qualified in the content area of reading or be able to become Highly Qualified in the content area of reading within 60 days from being hired. EXPERIENCE : Must have completed a program of study in education from an accredited college or university. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Principal DAYS PER YEAR: 190 SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Paid on the Certified Personnel Salary Schedule EMPLOYEE GROUP: Certified APPLICATION PROCEDURES: Must apply online by completing an "Certified" application and adding this specific job to your online application. FLSA STATU

2026-2027 Numeracy Coach JobID: 17638 Position Type: Certified - Elementary School Date Posted: 2/20/2026 Location: LADYS ISLAND ELEMENTARY (High-Needs School) Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION PURPOSE: The Numeracy Coach will empower teachers by working directly with all stakeholders to bring about improvements in the classroom/school that will positively impact student achievement. s='title' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> 2026-2027 JROTC - Senior Military Instructor at Battery Creek High School JobID: 17424 Position Type: Classified Staff Date Posted: 11/17/2025 Location: BATTERY CREEK HIGH Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION PURPOSE: Teach, coach and mentor JROTC Cadets to improve their learning and leadership capabilities, and make them better citizens and community leaders, administer and manage the JROTC program effectively according to law, regulations, policies and principles. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Actively recruit students to join JROTC. Achieve desired learning goals by teaching prescribed JROTC subjects listed in the Program of Instruction. Modify and improve given instruction by remaining continuously alert to new and alternative instructional methods and motivational techniques. Maintain supply facilities in strict compliance with individual service security regulations. Comply with service and school district safety guidelines in all instances when instructing, supervising, monitoring, and accompanying or escorting cadets. Maintain student records as prescribed in service regulations and local policies. Prepare unit for formal inspections. Counsel cadets on their performance. Conduct a public affairs program directed at school and community to further enhance understanding of the JROTC program. Supervise/conduct training at JROTC summer camp and other duties prescribed by camp commander. Assist cadets in applying for Senior ROTC scholarships and service academy appointments. Set the appropriate example in decorum and cooperativeness, positive attitude and consistency for cadets and colleagues. Draw, maintain and account for all property issued to the school; ensure sensitive items of equipment are secured. Organize, supervise, and develop extracurricular activities to include color guard, drill teams, and special programs. Maintain and manage all required budgets. Supervise student fund raising activities. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Ability to communicate effectively, both verbal and written. Retired Commissioned Officer for senior instructor or retired Non-Commissioned Officer for assistant instructor. Must be JROTC certified by appropriate military service. EXPERIENCE : Must be retired from the appropriate military service. Exhibits a professional and caring manner to students and parents; efficient, knowledgeable and mission oriented with faculty, administration, and other district personnel. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. If you have previously submitted an internal application, log on to your existing application and add the current vacancies of interest. Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources. PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Job.JobDescription: 'p17424_'> Teacher/Head Football Coach - Certification Area TBD JobID: 17424 Position Type: High School Teaching/ Subject Area (TBD) Date Posted: 2/26/2026 Location: Timberland High Closing Date: 03/17/2026 Additional Information: Show/Hide Compensation Structure: Base Salary: Determined by a valid South Carolina Teaching Certificate. Extended Contract Supplement: Includes 50 additional days calculated at the teacher's daily rate. Coaching Supplement - 2A: $8,000 - $11,300 (based on 020 years of verified experience), plus $165 for each additional year (pro-rated for the remaining of the year). Employment Note: Depending on the teacher/coach selected for hire, the candidate may begin as a temporary employee for the remainder of the 2025-2026 school year. Documents are not accepted otherwise. Negative TB test result - upload if available or provide with onboarding documents. TB Test result must be submitted at new hire orientation (no exceptions). General Definition of Work The Academic Assistant Coach supports the Academic Coach with assigned students by assisting with instructional activities, administering and reinforcing academic lessons, and guiding independent, enrichment, and remedial work to promote student success. The role includes monitoring students to ensure a safe and positive learning environment, maintaining accurate records and clerical support, and adhering to district policies and school expectations. Through direct instruction and support, the position contributes to students’ academic achievement, interpersonal development, and daily living skills within a structured educational setting. Qualifications Ability to assist students with instructional activities and lesson assignments Ability to support testing, homework, and make-up work as directed Ability to monitor and supervise students to ensure a safe learning environment Ability to guide students in independent, enrichment, and remedial activities Basic organizational and clerical skills, including record keeping and copying Ability to follow school schedules, district policies, and professional standards Effective communication and interpersonal skills when working with students and staff Salary/Status Support 3, 190 days/ 7.5 hours per day Reports To Principal Place of Work The normal place of work is on the premises used by Rock Hill Schools. Rock Hill Schools reserves the right to require you to work at such other place(s) as it may reasonably require from time to time. Essential Functions Assist with administering tests, homework assignments, make-up work, etc. for the purpose of supporting in the instructional process for the assigned students. Assist assigned students with lesson assignments and/or activities for the purpose of presenting and/or reinforcing learning concepts. Guides students in independent study (e.g. enrichment work, remedial work, etc.) for the purpose of ensuring students’ success. Instruct students for the purpose of improving their success in academic, interpersonal and daily living skills through a defined course of study. Meets expectations set for all personnel (e.g. school schedule, dress code, performance) for the purpose of maintaining safe and efficient school operations within District guidelines and policies. Monitors assigned student(s) according to predefined schedule in classroom for the purpose of ensuring a safe and positive learning environment. Performs record-keeping and clerical functions relating to the assigned student (e.g. scheduling, copying, etc.) for the purpose of supporting the assigned student. Comply with district policies, schedules, and professional expectations. Other duties as assigned Knowledge, Skills, Abilities Knowledge of basic instructional practices and classroom procedures Knowledge of student supervision and safety practices in an educational setting Ability to assist students with lessons, assignments, testing, and make-up work under teacher direction Ability to reinforce learning concepts and support independent, enrichment, and remedial activities Ability to provide instructional support that promotes academic achievement, interpersonal development, and daily living skills Ability to monitor assigned students and maintain a safe, positive learning environment Skill in basic record keeping and clerical tasks related to student support Ability to follow school schedules, district policies, and professional standards Effective communication and interpersonal skills when working with students, teachers, and staff Operating within a defined budget; and utilizing limited resources from other work units as needed to perform job functions Education and Experience All Instructional ParaProfessionals are required to meet one of the following three requirements prior to beginning employment. Transcripts and/or documentation must be uploaded to the application Minimum employment requirements; high school diploma; At least two years of study (60 semester hours) at an accredited institution of higher education; or An associate’s degree (or higher); or An approved state or local academic assessment. A combination of education, training, and experience that demonstrates the knowledge and abilities required may be considered Special Requirements Must have and maintain a valid Driver’s License or ability to provide own transportation Physical Requirements/Work Conditions Regularly required to stand; walk; use hands and fingers to handle, or feel objects, tools, or controls; and talk or hear Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus Frequently required to reach with hands and arms and occasionally required to sit Frequently squat, stoop or kneel, reach above the head and reach forward Frequently bend or twist at the neck and trunk more than the average person while performing the duties of this position Frequent interruptions and immediate attention to unexpected situations are expected Will regularly work indoors and may occasionally work outdoors The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate Disclaimer The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees in this job. Job description is sourced from employee interviews, internal documents, representative job descriptions in similar districts, and other state/federal agencies. The district reserves the right to modify the job description based on the needs of the district. Minimum Qualifications: SC certification in Physical Education with a strong GPA in relevant course work Outstanding verbal and written communication skills Knowledge of effective and innovative instructional and student evaluation techniques Experience working with high school students preferred Knowledge of instructional technology and its applications to the teaching of Physical Education and Health Willingness to work with other teachers to promote interdisciplinary studies Skill in interpersonal relations with pupils, parents, teachers, staff and the general public Outstanding references from current and former supervisors Satisfactory background report The Strength & Conditioning Coach is a separate stipend. Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources. Employment Status: Exempt PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. Position Title: Middle School Head Coach Department: Athletics Reports To: Athletic Director SUMMARY: To plan organize, coordinate and supervise the program in such a way as to produce a team that will be competitive in their classification and conference. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES . Assists the athletic director in selection and evaluation of assistant coaches and makes recommendations to the athletic director for assistant coaches. Assumes responsibility for checking all practice areas each day and removes hazardous objects from the practice area. Develops a positive public relations program in the school and the community. Makes recommendations to the athletic director for purchasing football equipment. Provides supervision in the locker rooms before and after practice.. Provides the latest and up-to-date medical procedures for the treatment of athletic injuries. The coach will at all times conduct himself in a manner that will set a good example for students to follow on and off the field. Assumes responsibility with the athletic director for eligibility of participants and insurance coverage of team members. Develops his own schedule with the approval of the athletic director. Assumes responsibility for knowing the latest football rules, South Carolina High School League rules and regulations and School District policies regarding interscholastic sports. Performs other duties as may be assigned by the Supervisor. JOB SPECIFICATIONS SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: All coaches in the school’s program and supervises students of particular program. Carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the district's policies and applicable laws. QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty in a pleasant, professional, efficient manner. Must be reliable, responsible and dependable and fulfill obligations. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE: To be decided by Executive Director and/or Building Principal. CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS: Valid South Carolina Teaching Certificate, if applicable. LANGUAGE SKILLS: Ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. Ability to speak effectively before administrators, staff, parents and students. MATHEMATICAL SKILLS : Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, commissions, proportions, percentages, area, circumference, and volume. Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry. REASONING ABILITY: Ability to interpret and apply common sense understanding in carrying out instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram or schedule form. Ability to analyze information and use logic to address work related issues and problems. Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists. OTHER SKILLS and ABILITIES: Ability to apply knowledge of current research and theory in specific field. Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with students, staff and the community. Ability to communicate clearly and concisely both in oral and written form. Ability to perform duties with awareness of all district requirements and Board of Education policies. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to sit; occasionally walk and stand; travel from building to other sites. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. The employee is required to be able to hear conversations in quiet environments. WORK ENVIRONMENT: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee regularly works inside. The employee must be able to meet deadlines with severe time constraints. The employee is frequently will work irregular or extended work hours while performing the duties of this job. The employee is directly responsible for safety, well-being and work output of students and coaches. The noise level in the work environment varies from quiet to loud. TERM OF EMPLOYMENT: Seasonal Athletic Supplements Pay Scale EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated annually according to Board Policy. The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the American with Disabilities Act (A.D.A.) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. Updated 2019

Job.JobDescription: Position Title: Head Coach Department: Athletics Reports To: Athletic Director SUMMARY: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES . Coaches individuals in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. Plans and schedules a regular program of practice in season. Oversees the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present. Maintains accurate statistics, records, and results of the season. Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times, and establishes and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual students. Maintains competency in rules, rule interpretations, meet procedures, coaching techniques, and general information about all aspects of the sport. Establishes performance criteria for eligibility in interscholastic competition in this sport and is consistent in establishing criteria for eligibility in competition. Adheres to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention and follow up. Works closely with the athletics director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Recommends purchase of equipment, supplies, and uniforms. Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork. Accounts for all equipment or delegates the responsibility. Develops a consistent and positive public communications procedure through the school and media to inform the parents and the general public about the athletic program. Performs other duties as may be assigned by the Supervisor. JOB SPECIFICATIONS SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: Supervises students of particular program. Carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the district's policies and applicable laws.
